       # WP-2026-129: Fallback to opencode for the Manager\n252:             return \"opencode\"\n253: \n254:     def _get_manager_model(self) -> str | None:\n255:         \"\"\"Get the model override for MANAGER from role_models.\"\"\"\n256:         try:\n257:             from agents_config import get_model_for_role, load_agents_config\n258: \n259:             config = load_agents_config(self.project_root)\n260:             return get_model_for_role(\"MANAGER\", config)\n261:         except Exception:\n262:             return None\n263: \n264:     def _get_canonical_files(self) -> list[Path]:\n265:         \"\"\"Get list of canonical collaboration files to attach to OpenCode review.\"\"\"\n266:         collaboration_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n267:         files = [\n268:             collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\",\n269:             collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\",\n270:             collaboration_dir / \"TURN.md\",\n271:             collaboration_dir / \"STATE.md\",\n272:         ]\n273:         ticket_id = self.state_ingest._get_active_ticket_id()\n274:         if ticket_id:\n275:             plan_file = collaboration_dir / f\"PLAN_{ticket_id}.md\"\n276:             audit_file = collaboration_dir / f\"AUDIT_{ticket_id}.md\"\n277:             if plan_file.exists():\n278:                 files.append(plan_file)\n279:             if audit_file.exists():\n280:                 files.append(audit_file)\n281:         return [f for f in files if f.exists()]\n282: \n283:     def _get_active_ticket_id(self) -> str | None:\n284:         \"\"\"Read active ticket ID from work_plan.md.\"\"\"\n285:         work_plan = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n286:         if not work_plan.exists():\n287:             return None\n288:         content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n289:         # Look for **- ID:** WP-XXXX-XXX pattern\n290:         match = re.search(r\"\\*\\*ID:\\*\\*\\s*(WP-\\d{4}-\\d+)\", content)\n291:         if match:\n292:             return match.group(1)\n293:         return None\n294: \n295:     def _read_deliverable_type(self) -> str:\n296:         \"\"\"Read the deliverable_type from work_plan.md. Defaults to 'code'.\"\"\"\n297:         work_plan = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / \"work_plan.md\"\n298:         if not work_plan.exists():\n299:             return \"code\"\n300:         try:\n301:             content = work_plan.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n302:             match = re.search(\n303:                 r\"^\\s*-\\s*\\*\\*deliverable_type:\\*\\*\\s*(\\S+)\",\n304:                 content,\n305:                 re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE,\n306:             )\n307:             if not match:\n308:                 return \"code\"\n309:             value = match.group(1).strip().lower()\n310:             if \"+\" in value:\n311:                 return \"mixed\"\n312:             valid_types = {\"code\", \"documentation\", \"research\", \"analysis\", \"mixed\"}\n313:             if value not in valid_types:\n314:                 return \"code\"\n315:             return value\n316:         except Exception:\n317:             return \"code\"\n318: \n319:     def _read_canonical(self, name: str) -> str:\n320:         path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n321:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else \"\"\n322: \n323:     def _read_canonical_optional(self, name: str) -> str | None:\n324:         path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\" / name\n325:         return path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") if path.exists() else None\n326: \n327:     def _extract_ticket_section(self, ticket_id: str) -> str:\n328:         content = self.state_ingest._read_canonical(\"execution_log.md\")\n329:         pattern = rf\"### {re.escape(ticket_id)}.*?(?=\\n### WP-|\\Z)\"\n330:         match = re.search(pattern, content, re.DOTALL)\n331:         return (\n332:             match.group(0)\n333:             if match\n334:             else f\"[execution_log section for {ticket_id} not found]\"\n335:         )\n336: \n337:     def _git_diff_stat(self) -> str:\n338:         try:\n339:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n340:             result = subprocess.run(\n341:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"--stat\", \"origin/main...HEAD\"],\n342:                 capture_output=True,\n343:                 text=True,\n344:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n345:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n346:                 timeout=10,\n347:             )\n348:             if result.returncode == 0:\n349:                 return result.stdout or \"[git diff --stat empty]\"\n350:             result = subprocess.run(\n351:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"--stat\", \"HEAD\"],\n352:                 capture_output=True,\n353:                 text=True,\n354:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n355:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n356:                 timeout=10,\n357:             )\n358:             return \"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable, using HEAD fallback]\\n\" + (\n359:                 result.stdout or \"[git diff --stat empty]\"\n360:             )\n361:         except Exception as e:\n362:             return f\"[Error fetching git diff --stat: {e}]\"\n363: \n364:     def _build_diff_for_files_likely_touched(\n365:         self, ticket_id: str, budget_bytes: int\n366:     ) -> str:\n367:         try:\n368:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n369:             result = subprocess.run(\n370:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"origin/main...HEAD\"],\n371:                 capture_output=True,\n372:                 text=True,\n373:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n374:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n375:                 timeout=15,\n376:             )\n377:             warning_prefix = \"\"\n378:             if result.returncode != 0:\n379:                 result = subprocess.run(\n380:                     [git_bin, \"diff\", \"HEAD\"],\n381:                     capture_output=True,\n382:                     text=True,\n383:                     encoding=\"utf-8\",\n384:                     cwd=self.project_root,\n385:                     timeout=15,\n386:                 )\n387:                 warning_prefix = \"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable, using git diff HEAD fallback]\\n\"\n388:             diff = warning_prefix + (result.stdout or \"\")\n389:             diff_bytes = diff.encode(\"utf-8\")\n390:             if len(diff_bytes) <= budget_bytes:\n391:                 return diff\n392:             truncated = diff_bytes[:budget_bytes].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n393:             return truncated + \"\\n\\n[diff truncado por budget]\"\n394:         except Exception as e:\n395:             return f\"[Error fetching git diff: {e}]\"\n396: \n397:     def _git_provenance(self) -> str:\n398:         try:\n399:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n400:             result = subprocess.run(\n401:                 [\n402:                     git_bin,\n403:                     \"log\",\n404:                     \"origin/main..HEAD\",\n405:                     \"--format=%H %ai %an\",\n406:                     \"--no-merges\",\n407:                     \"-1\",\n408:                 ],\n409:                 capture_output=True,\n410:                 text=True,\n411:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n412:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n413:                 timeout=10,\n414:             )\n415:             if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():\n416:                 return result.stdout.strip()\n417:             result = subprocess.run(\n418:                 [git_bin, \"log\", \"HEAD\", \"--format=%H %ai %an\", \"--no-merges\", \"-1\"],\n419:                 capture_output=True,\n420:                 text=True,\n421:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n422:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n423:                 timeout=10,\n424:             )\n425:             line = result.stdout.strip() or \"[no commits found]\"\n426:             return f\"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable] {line}\"\n427:         except Exception as e:\n428:             return f\"[Error fetching git provenance: {e}]\"\n429: \n430:     def _rubric_for_type(self, dtype: str, ticket_id: str) -> str:\n431:         if dtype == \"code\":\n432:             return (\n433:                 f\"Review code ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n434:                 f\"Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. \"\n435:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n436:             )\n437:         elif dtype == \"mixed\":\n438:             return (\n439:                 f\"Review mixed ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n440:                 f\"Verify code correctness, tests, and style guides, and additionally verify \"\n441:                 f\"that all declared non-code deliverables exist, are well-structured, and are fully complete. \"\n442:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n443:             )\n444:         else:  # documentation, research, analysis\n445:             return (\n446:                 f\"Review non-code {dtype} ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n447:                 f\"Since this is a non-code deliverable, focus strictly on the clarity, depth, correctness, \"\n448:                 f\"structure, and completeness of the requested document deliverables. \"\n449:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n450:             )\n451: \n452:     def _build_review_prompt(self, ticket_id: str, dtype: str) -> str:\n453:         # P1-2: work_plan, STATE, TURN (intocables)\n454:         sections = [\n455:             (name, self.state_ingest._read_canonical(name))\n456:             for name in (\"work_plan.md\", \"STATE.md\", \"TURN.md\")\n457:         ]\n458: \n459:         # P3: execution_log section\n460:         sections.append(\n461:             (\n462:                 \"execution_log.md (ticket section)\",\n463:                 self._extract_ticket_section(ticket_id),\n464:             )\n465:         )\n466: \n467:         # P4: PLAN + AUDIT opcionales\n468:         for name in (f\"PLAN_{ticket_id}.md\", f\"AUDIT_{ticket_id}.md\"):\n469:             content = self.state_ingest._read_canonical_optional(name)\n470:             if content is not None:\n471:                 sections.append((name, content))\n472: \n473:         # Calculate used bytes\n474:         used = sum(len(c.encode(\"utf-8\")) for _, c in sections)\n475: \n476:         hard_cap_bytes = 80 * 1024\n477:         canonical_budget = 60 * 1024\n478: \n479:         # P4.5: git provenance (compact, inserted before diff)\n480:         provenance = self._git_provenance()\n481:         sections.append((\"git provenance\", provenance))\n482:         used += len(provenance.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n483: \n484:         # P5: diff con budget restante\n485:         if used < canonical_budget:\n486:             remaining = hard_cap_bytes - used\n487:             diff = self._build_diff_for_files_likely_touched(ticket_id, remaining)\n488:             if diff:\n489:                 sections.append((\"git diff (Files Likely Touched)\", diff))\n490:         else:\n491:             sections.append(\n492:                 (\n493:                     \"git diff --stat\",\n494:                     self._git_diff_stat()\n495:                     + \"\\n[diff omitido por budget. Read files directly if needed.]\",\n496:                 )\n497:             )\n498: \n499:         # Compose\n500:         parts = [self._rubric_for_type(dtype, ticket_id)]\n501:         for name, content in sections:\n502:             parts.append(f\"\\n--- {name} ---\\n{content}\")\n503: \n504:         # WP-2026-127: Add skill filtering by role\n505:         current_role = self._get_current_role()\n506:         allowed_skills = self.skill_resolver.filter_skills_for_prompt(\n507:             current_role, include_metadata=False\n508:         )\n509:         parts.append(\n510:             f\"\\n--- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE {current_role} ---\\n{allowed_skills}\"\n511:         )\n512: \n513:         parts.append(\n514:             \"\\n--- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\\n\"\n515:             \"Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\\n\"\n516:             \"Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\\n\"\n517:             \"- PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\\n\"\n518:             \"- review_queue.md, notifications.md\\n\"\n519:             \"- archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\\n\"\n520:             \"- .session_state.json\\n\"\n521:         )\n522:         parts.append(\n523:             \"\\n--- INSTRUCTIONS ---\\n\"\n524:             \"This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\\n\\n\"\n525:             \"Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion \"\n526:             \"are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed \"\n527:             \"by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\\n\\n\"\n528:             \"If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\\n\"\n529:             \"DECISION: APPROVE\\n\\n\"\n530:             \"If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure \"\n531:             \"(headings included) before the final line:\\n\\n\"\n532:             \"## SUMMARY\\n\"\n533:             \"<one line: why the ticket is not approved>\\n\\n\"\n534:             \"## BLOCKERS\\n\"\n535:             \"- <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\\n\\n\"\n536:             \"## SUGGESTIONS\\n\"\n537:             \"- <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\\n\\n\"\n538:             \"DECISION: CHANGES\\n\\n\"\n539:             \"If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\\n\"\n540:             \"DECISION: INSPECT\\n\"\n541:         )\n542:         return \"\\n\".join(parts)\n543: \n544:     def _run_legacy_manager_review(\n545:         self,\n546:         *,\n547:         ticket_id: str,\n548:         manager_executable: Path,\n549:         timeout_seconds: int,\n550:     ) -> tuple[str, str, int]:\n551:         \"\"\"Legacy Manager review route. Preserved for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n552:         exe_str = str(manager_executable)\n553:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_str.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n554:             cmd_candidate = Path(exe_str).with_suffix(\".cmd\")\n555:             bat_candidate = Path(exe_str).with_suffix(\".bat\")\n556:             if cmd_candidate.exists():\n557:                 exe_str = str(cmd_candidate)\n558:             elif bat_candidate.exists():\n559:                 exe_str = str(bat_candidate)\n560: \n561:         command = [exe_str, \"review\", ticket_id]\n562:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_str.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n563:             command = [\n564:                 \"powershell.exe\",\n565:                 \"-NoProfile\",\n566:                 \"-ExecutionPolicy\",\n567:                 \"Bypass\",\n568:                 \"-File\",\n569:                 *command,\n570:             ]\n571: \n572:         result = subprocess.run(\n573:             command,\n574:             capture_output=True,\n575:             text=True,\n576:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n577:             cwd=self.project_root,\n578:             env=self._review_env(),\n579:             timeout=timeout_seconds,\n580:         )\n581:         return result.stdout or \"\", result.stderr or \"\", result.returncode\n582: \n583:     def _run_opencode_review(  # noqa: C901\n584:         self,\n585:         *,\n586:         ticket_id: str,\n587:         prompt: str,\n588:         attempt: int = 1,\n589:         manager_executable: Path | None = None,\n590:         timeout_seconds: int,\n591:     ) -> tuple[str, str, int]:\n592:         \"\"\"OpenCode review route using manager agent spec with context prompt.\n593: \n594:         Transporte: el contexto completo se escribe a un review packet en\n595:         .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md y el prompt\n596:         posicional es corto, indicando al Manager que lo lea. Evita el flag\n597:         --file (array que consume el mensaje) y el limite de longitud de la\n598:         CLI.\n599:         \"\"\"\n600:         model = self._get_manager_model()\n601: \n602:         executable = str(manager_executable) if manager_executable else \"opencode\"\n603:         if os.name == \"nt\" and executable == \"opencode\":\n604:             executable = \"opencode.cmd\"\n605: \n606:         exe_full = shutil.which(executable) or executable\n607:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_full.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n608:             cmd_candidate = Path(exe_full).with_suffix(\".cmd\")\n609:             bat_candidate = Path(exe_full).with_suffix(\".bat\")\n610:             if cmd_candidate.exists():\n611:                 exe_full = str(cmd_candidate)\n612:             elif bat_candidate.exists():\n613:                 exe_full = str(bat_candidate)\n614: \n615:         # Review packet: el contexto completo del review se escribe a un\n616:         # archivo dentro del repo; el Manager lo lee con sus herramientas.\n617:         # El prompt posicional es corto (sin metacaracteres de cmd.exe).\n618:         packet_path = self._get_review_packet_path(ticket_id, attempt)\n619:         packet_path.write_text(prompt, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n620:         packet_rel = packet_path.relative_to(self.project_root).as_posix()\n621: \n622:         review_message = (\n623:             f\"Revisa {ticket_id} leyendo el archivo {packet_rel} con tus \"\n624:             \"herramientas de lectura. Termina con exactamente \"\n625:             \"DECISION: APPROVE o DECISION: CHANGES.\"\n626:         )\n627: \n628:         if not review_message.isascii():\n629:             raise ValueError(\n630:                 \"Review message must be ASCII for Windows command transport.\"\n631:             )\n632: \n633:         cmd_args = [exe_full, \"run\", \"--agent\", \"manager\"]\n634:         if model:\n635:             cmd_args.extend([\"--model\", model])\n636:         if self._supports_json_format:\n637:             cmd_args.extend([\"--format\", \"json\"])\n638:         cmd_args.append(review_message)\n639: \n640:         use_shell = False\n641:         if os.name == \"nt\" and (\n642:             exe_full.lower().endswith(\".cmd\")\n643:             or exe_full.lower().endswith(\".bat\")\n644:             or \"opencode\" in exe_full.lower()\n645:         ):\n646:             use_shell = True\n647: \n648:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_full.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n649:             cmd_args = [\n650:                 \"powershell.exe\",\n651:                 \"-NoProfile\",\n652:                 \"-ExecutionPolicy\",\n653:                 \"Bypass\",\n654:                 \"-File\",\n655:                 *cmd_args,\n656:             ]\n657: \n658:         try:\n659:             # En Windows, shell=True con una lista hace que cmd.exe trate\n660:             # args[1:] como argumentos del propio shell: opencode arrancaria\n661:             # sin ninguno. list2cmdline produce el string correcto.\n662:             run_args = subprocess.list2cmdline(cmd_args) if use_shell else cmd_args\n663:             result = subprocess.run(\n664:                 run_args,\n665:                 capture_output=True,\n666:                 text=True,\n667:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n668:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n669:                 timeout=timeout_seconds,\n670:                 shell=use_shell,\n671:             )\n672:         except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError, OSError) as exc:\n673:             err_msg = f\"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}\"\n674:             if isinstance(exc, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):\n675:                 err_msg = f\"TimeoutExpired: {exc}\"\n676:             return \"\", err_msg, 1\n677: \n678:         return result.stdout or \"\", result.stderr or \"\", result.returncode\n679: \n680:     def _load_review_config(self) -> dict:\n681:         \"\"\"Load review configuration from agents.json with WP-2026-106 defaults.\n682: \n683:         Before: max_attempts was 2 (legacy default).\n684:         During: Reads agents.json manager_review section, applies WP-2026-106 threshold of 5.\n685:         After: Returns config dict with max_attempts=5 for HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n686:         \"\"\"\n687:         config_path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n688:         defaults = {\n689:             \"timeout_seconds\": 180,\n690:             \"max_attempts\": 5,  # WP-2026-106: elevated from 3 to 5 for HUMAN_GATE\n691:             \"retry_backoff_multiplier\": 2.0,\n692:         }\n693:         if not config_path.exists():\n694:             return defaults\n695:         try:\n696:             data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n697:             cfg = data.get(\"manager_review\", {})\n698:             return {\n699:                 \"timeout_seconds\": int(\n700:                     cfg.get(\"timeout_seconds\", defaults[\"timeout_seconds\"])\n701:                 ),\n702:                 \"max_attempts\": int(cfg.get(\"max_attempts\", defaults[\"max_attempts\"])),\n703:                 \"retry_backoff_multiplier\": float(\n704:                     cfg.get(\n705:                         \"retry_backoff_multiplier\", defaults[\"retry_backoff_multiplier\"]\n706:                     )\n707:                 ),\n708:             }\n709:         except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, TypeError):\n710:             return defaults\n711: \n712:     def _get_review_log_path(self, ticket_id: str) -> Path:\n713:         \"\"\"Get the review log directory for a ticket.\n714: \n715:         Before: Requires ticket_id string.\n716:         During: Creates directory structure under .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/.\n717:         After: Returns Path object for the ticket's review log directory.\n718:         \"\"\"\n719:         reviews_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"reviews\"\n720:         ticket_dir = reviews_dir / ticket_id\n721:         ticket_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n722:         return ticket_dir\n723: \n724:     def _get_review_packet_path(self, ticket_id: str, attempt: int) -> Path:\n725:         \"\"\"Get the canonical review packet path for a ticket attempt.\"\"\"\n726:         packets_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"review_packets\"\n727:         packets_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n728:         return packets_dir / f\"{ticket_id}_attempt-{attempt}.md\"\n729: \n730:     def _persist_review_attempt(\n731:         self,\n732:         ticket_id: str,\n733:         attempt: int,\n734:         stdout: str,\n735:         stderr: str,\n736:         decision: ReviewDecision,\n737:         review_packet_path: Path | None = None,\n738:         parse_method: str = \"\",\n739:         transport_ok: bool = True,\n740:         transport_error: str = \"\",\n741:     ) -> Path:\n742:         \"\"\"Persist a review attempt to attempt-N.md idempotently.\n743: \n744:         Before: Requires ticket_id, attempt number, stdout, stderr, and decision.\n745:         During: Writes review content to .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/attempt-N.md,\n746:                 overwriting any existing file with the same attempt number.\n747:         After: Returns the Path to the persisted review file.\n748:         \"\"\"\n749:         ticket_dir = self._get_review_log_path(ticket_id)\n750:         attempt_file = ticket_dir / f\"attempt-{attempt}.md\"\n751: \n752:         # Build structured review content\n753:         content_parts = [\n754:             f\"# Review Attempt {attempt}\",\n755:             \"\",\n756:             f\"## Ticket: {ticket_id}\",\n757:             f\"## Decision: {decision.value.upper()}\",\n758:             f\"## Parse Method: {parse_method or 'unknown'}\",\n759:             f\"## Transport OK: {transport_ok}\",\n760:             \"\",\n761:             \"## Review Packet\",\n762:             \"\",\n763:             str(review_packet_path.relative_to(self.project_root).as_posix())\n764:             if review_packet_path is not None\n765:             else \"[not recorded]\",\n766:             \"\",\n767:             \"## Transport Error\",\n768:             \"\",\n769:             transport_error or \"[none]\",\n770:             \"\",\n771:             \"## STDOUT\",\n772:             \"\",\n773:             stdout or \"[empty]\",\n774:             \"\",\n775:             \"## STDERR\",\n776:             \"\",\n777:             stderr or \"[empty]\",\n778:             \"\",\n779:         ]\n780: \n781:         # Parse and add structured sections for CHANGES decisions\n782:         if decision == ReviewDecision.CHANGES:\n783:             structured = self._parse_changes_structure(stdout)\n784:             content_parts.extend(\n785:                 [\n786:                     \"\",\n787:                     \"## SUMMARY\",\n788:                     \"\",\n789:                     structured.get(\"summary\", \"[no summary provided]\"),\n790:                     \"\",\n791:                     \"## BLOCKERS\",\n792:                     \"\",\n793:                     structured.get(\"blockers\", \"[no blockers provided]\"),\n794:                     \"\",\n795:                     \"## SUGGESTIONS\",\n796:                     \"\",\n797:                     structured.get(\"suggestions\", \"[no suggestions provided]\"),\n798:                     \"\",\n799:                 ]\n800:             )\n801: \n802:         attempt_file.write_text(\"\\n\".join(content_parts), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n803:         return attempt_file\n804: \n805:     def _parse_changes_structure(self, stdout: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n806:         \"\"\"Parse structured sections from CHANGES review output.\n807: \n808:         Before: Requires stdout string from Manager review.\n809:         During: Extracts SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS sections using regex patterns.\n810:         After: Returns dict with 'summary', 'blockers', 'suggestions' keys (defaults if not found).\n811:         \"\"\"\n812:         result = {\"summary\": \"\", \"blockers\": \"\", \"suggestions\": \"\"}\n813: \n814:         # Pattern for ## SUMMARY section\n815:         summary_match = re.search(\n816:             r\"##\\s*SUMMARY\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:BLOCKERS|SUGGESTIONS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n817:             stdout,\n818:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n819:         )\n820:         if summary_match:\n821:             result[\"summary\"] = summary_match.group(1).strip()\n822: \n823:         # Pattern for ## BLOCKERS section\n824:         blockers_match = re.search(\n825:             r\"##\\s*BLOCKERS\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:SUMMARY|SUGGESTIONS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n826:             stdout,\n827:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n828:         )\n829:         if blockers_match:\n830:             result[\"blockers\"] = blockers_match.group(1).strip()\n831: \n832:         # Pattern for ## SUGGESTIONS section\n833:         suggestions_match = re.search(\n834:             r\"##\\s*SUGGESTIONS\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:SUMMARY|BLOCKERS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n835:             stdout,\n836:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n837:         )\n838:         if suggestions_match:\n839:             result[\"suggestions\"] = suggestions_match.group(1).strip()\n840: \n841:         return result\n842: \n843:     def _validate_changes_structure(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:\n844:         \"\"\"Validate that CHANGES response has required structure.\n845: \n846:         Before: Requires stdout string from Manager review.\n847:         During: Checks for presence of SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS, and DECISION: CHANGES.\n848:         After: Returns (is_valid, missing_sections) tuple.\n849:         \"\"\"\n850:         missing = []\n851:         stdout_upper = stdout.upper()\n852: \n853:         # Require the exact heading form, not a loose word in prose, so a review\n854:         # that merely mentions \"summary\" cannot pass structure validation.\n855:         if \"## SUMMARY\" not in stdout_upper:\n856:             missing.append(\"SUMMARY\")\n857:         if \"## BLOCKERS\" not in stdout_upper:\n858:             missing.append(\"BLOCKERS\")\n859:         if \"## SUGGESTIONS\" not in stdout_upper:\n860:             missing.append(\"SUGGESTIONS\")\n861:         if \"DECISION: CHANGES\" not in stdout_upper:\n862:             missing.append(\"DECISION: CHANGES\")\n863: \n864:         return len(missing) == 0, missing\n865: \n866:     def _generate_human_review_report(\n867:         self,\n868:         ticket_id: str,\n869:         review_attempts: list[dict],\n870:         last_decision: ReviewDecision,\n871:     ) -> Path:\n872:         \"\"\"Generate human_review_report.md from template at 5th rejection.\n873: \n874:         Before: Requires ticket_id, list of review attempt payloads, and last decision.\n875:         During: Reads template from .agent/templates/human_review_report.md,\n876:                 fills placeholders with consolidated review data,\n877:                 writes to .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/human_review_report.md.\n878:         After: Returns Path to generated report file.\n879:         \"\"\"\n880:         template_path = (\n881:             self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"templates\" / \"human_review_report.md\"\n882:         )\n883: \n884:         # Load template\n885:         if template_path.exists():\n886:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n887:         else:\n888:             # Fallback inline template\n889:             template = \"\"\"# Human Review Report\n890: \n891: ## Ticket\n892: - **Ticket ID:** {{ticket_id}}\n893: - **Generated at:** {{generated_at}}\n894: - **Review attempts:** {{review_attempt_count}}\n895: \n896: ## Summary\n897: {{summary}}\n898: \n899: ## Decision Context\n900: - **Last decision:** {{last_decision}}\n901: - **Escalation reason:** {{escalation_reason}}\n902: \n903: ## Consolidated Blockers\n904: {{blockers}}\n905: \n906: ## Notes\n907: {{notes}}\n908: \"\"\"\n909: \n910:         # Consolidate blockers from all attempts\n911:         all_blockers = []\n912:         for attempt in review_attempts:\n913:             payload = attempt.get(\"payload\", {})\n914:             blockers = payload.get(\"blockers\", \"\")\n915:             if blockers:\n916:                 all_blockers.append(\n917:                     f\"### Attempt {attempt.get('attempt', '?')}\\n{blockers}\"\n918:                 )\n919: \n920:         # Build summary\n921:         summary_lines = [\n922:             f\"Ticket {ticket_id} reached HUMAN_GATE after {len(review_attempts)} consecutive CHANGES decisions.\",\n923:             \"\",\n924:             \"## Review History\",\n925:             \"\",\n926:         ]\n927:         for attempt in review_attempts:\n928:             payload = attempt.get(\"payload\", {})\n929:             summary_lines.append(\n930:                 f\"- Attempt {attempt.get('attempt', '?')}: \"\n931:                 f\"exit_code={payload.get('exit_code', 'N/A')}, \"\n932:                 f\"duration={payload.get('duration_seconds', 'N/A')}s\"\n933:             )\n934: \n935:         # Fill template\n936:         report_content = (\n937:             template.replace(\"{{ticket_id}}\", ticket_id)\n938:             .replace(\"{{generated_at}}\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())\n939:             .replace(\"{{review_attempt_count}}\", str(len(review_attempts)))\n940:             .replace(\"{{summary}}\", \"\\n\".join(summary_lines))\n941:             .replace(\"{{last_decision}}\", last_decision.value.upper())\n942:             .replace(\n943:                 \"{{escalation_reason}}\",\n944:                 f\"Reached {len(review_attempts)} consecutive CHANGES decisions (threshold: 5)\",\n945:             )\n946:             .replace(\n947:                 \"{{blockers}}\",\n948:                 \"\\n\\n\".join(all_blockers)\n949:                 if all_blockers\n950:                 else \"[No structured blockers found]\",\n951:             )\n952:             .replace(\n953:                 \"{{notes}}\",\n954:                 \"This report was auto-generated when the review budget was exhausted. Human review required.\",\n955:             )\n956:         )\n957: \n958:         # Write report\n959:         ticket_dir = self._get_review_log_path(ticket_id)\n960:         report_path = ticket_dir / \"human_review_report.md\"\n961:         report_path.write_text(report_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n962: \n963:         return report_path\n964: \n965:     def _emit_review_attempt(\n966:         self,\n967:         ticket_id: str,\n968:         attempt: int,\n969:         timeout_s: int,\n970:         exit_code: int,\n971:         duration: float,\n972:         stdout: str,\n973:         decision: ReviewDecision | None = None,\n974:         review_log_path: Path | None = None,\n975:         parse_method: str = \"\",\n976:         transport_ok: bool = True,\n977:         transport_error: str = \"\",\n978:     ):\n979:         \"\"\"Emit a lightweight MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT event.\n980: \n981:         Before: Requires attempt metadata and optional decision/review_log_path.\n982:         During: Emits event with only review_log_path + a short stdout_tail.\n983:         After: Event is recorded in bus; full review content (stdout, stderr,\n984:             parsed blockers) is persisted separately in attempt-N.md, not the bus.\n985: \n986:         WP-2026-118: Fail-safe added - if emit() fails, log audibly and continue\n987:         without crashing the review cycle.\n988:         \"\"\"\n989:         try:\n990:             # WP-2026-106 B2: lightweight payload only. Full stdout/stderr and\n991:             # parsed blockers live in attempt-N.md; the bus keeps a pointer\n992:             # (review_log_path) plus a short stdout_tail for quick forensics.\n993:             payload = {\n994:                 \"attempt\": attempt,\n995:                 \"timeout_seconds\": timeout_s,\n996:                 \"exit_code\": exit_code,\n997:                 \"duration_seconds\": round(duration, 2),\n998:                 \"stdout_tail\": (stdout or \"\")[-500:],\n999:                 \"transport_ok\": transport_ok,\n1000:             }\n1001:             if decision:\n1002:                 payload[\"decision\"] = decision.value\n1003:             if review_log_path:\n1004:                 payload[\"review_log_path\"] = str(review_log_path)\n1005:             if parse_method:\n1006:                 payload[\"parse_method\"] = parse_method\n1007:             if transport_error:\n1008:                 payload[\"transport_error\"] = transport_error\n1009: \n1010:             self.event_bus.emit(\n1011:                 \"MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT\",\n1012:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1013:                 actor=\"MANAGER\",\n1014:                 payload=payload,\n1015:             )\n1016:         except Exception as exc:\n1017:             # WP-2026-118: Fail-safe - log the error audibly but don't crash\n1018:             print(\n1019:                 f\"[manager-review-bridge] FAIL-SAFE: event_bus.emit() failed for \"\n1020:                 f\"ticket {ticket_id}, attempt {attempt}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}. \"\n1021:                 f\"Review cycle continues; check attempt-{attempt}.md for full content.\",\n1022:                 file=sys.stderr,\n1023:             )\n1024: \n1025:     def _count_prior_changes_from_bus(self, ticket_id: str) -> int:\n1026:         \"\"\"Count consecutive trailing CHANGES decisions for a ticket from the bus.\n1027: \n1028:         Thin wrapper over bus.utils.count_trailing_changes (the single source\n1029:         of truth for this logic). Returns 0 if the bus is unreadable.\n1030:         \"\"\"\n1031:         try:\n1032:             events = self.event_bus.read_events(\n1033:                 ticket_id=ticket_id, event_type=\"REVIEW_DECISION\"\n1034:             )\n1035:         except Exception:\n1036:             return 0\n1037:         return count_trailing_changes(events)\n1038: \n1039:     def _parse_opencode_json_decision(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[ReviewDecision, str]:\n1040:         \"\"\"Parse OpenCode NDJSON output for DECISION: APPROVE|CHANGES|INSPECT pattern.\n1041: \n1042:         Returns (decision, parse_method) where parse_method is:\n1043:           - \"json_final_answer\" if decision extracted from phase:final_answer\n1044:           - \"json_last_text\" if decision extracted from last text event\n1045:           - \"json_no_decision\" if no decision found in any JSON event\n1046: \n1047:         Schema real de OpenCode:\n1048:         - Eventos type:\"text\" con part.text contienen el texto del modelo.\n1049:         - Eventos con phase:\"final_answer\" tienen autoridad superior.\n1050:         - Se prioriza el texto de final_answer sobre otros fragmentos.\n1051:         \"\"\"\n1052:         # First pass: look for final_answer phase (highest authority)\n1053:         final_answer_decision = self._extract_decision_from_text_events(\n1054:             stdout, require_final_answer=True\n1055:         )\n1056:         if final_answer_decision is not None:\n1057:             return final_answer_decision, \"json_final_answer\"\n1058: \n1059:         # Second pass: use last text event decision (no phase filter)\n1060:         last_text_decision = self._extract_decision_from_text_events(\n1061:             stdout, require_final_answer=False\n1062:         )\n1063:         if last_text_decision is not None:\n1064:             return last_text_decision, \"json_last_text\"\n1065: \n1066:         return ReviewDecision.INSPECT, \"json_no_decision\"\n1067: \n1068:     def _extract_decision_from_text_events(\n1069:         self, stdout: str, require_final_answer: bool\n1070:     ) -> ReviewDecision | None:\n1071:         \"\"\"Extract DECISION from text events, optionally filtering by phase.\n1072: \n1073:         Args:\n1074:             stdout: NDJSON output from OpenCode.\n1075:             require_final_answer: If True, only consider events with phase:\"final_answer\".\n1076: \n1077:         Returns:\n1078:             ReviewDecision if found, None otherwise.\n1079:         \"\"\"\n1080:         last_decision = None\n1081:         for line in stdout.splitlines():\n1082:             decision = self._extract_decision_from_single_line(\n1083:                 line, require_final_answer\n1084:             )\n1085:             if decision is not None:\n1086:                 if require_final_answer:\n1087:                     return decision\n1088:                 if last_decision is None:\n1089:                     last_decision = decision\n1090:         return last_decision\n1091: \n1092:     def _extract_decision_from_single_line(\n1093:         self, line: str, require_final_answer: bool\n1094:     ) -> ReviewDecision | None:\n1095:         \"\"\"Extract decision from a single NDJSON line.\n1096: \n1097:         Args:\n1098:             line: Single line of NDJSON output.\n1099:             require_final_answer: If True, only consider events with phase:\"final_answer\".\n1100: \n1101:         Returns:\n1102:             ReviewDecision if found, None otherwise.\n1103:         \"\"\"\n1104:         line = line.strip()\n1105:         if not line or not line.startswith(\"{\"):\n1106:             return None\n1107: \n1108:         try:\n1109:             event = json.loads(line)\n1110:         except json.JSONDecodeError:\n1111:             return None\n1112: \n1113:         if event.get(\"type\") != \"text\":\n1114:             return None\n1115: \n1116:         # Phase filter\n1117:         phase = event.get(\"phase\", \"\")\n1118:         if require_final_answer and phase != \"final_answer\":\n1119:             return None\n1120: \n1121:         # Extract text from part.text\n1122:         part = event.get(\"part\", {})\n1123:         if not isinstance(part, dict):\n1124:             return None\n1125:         text = part.get(\"text\", \"\")\n1126:         if not text:\n1127:             return None\n1128: \n1129:         text_upper = text.upper()\n1130:         if \"DECISION: CHANGES\" in text_upper:\n1131:             return ReviewDecision.CHANGES\n1132:         if \"DECISION: APPROVE\" in text_upper:\n1133:             return ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n1134:         if \"DECISION: INSPECT\" in text_upper:\n1135:             return ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n1136: \n1137:         return None\n1138: \n1139:     def _parse_opencode_decision_with_retry(\n1140:         self, stdout: str, stderr: str, max_retries: int = 2\n1141:     ) -> tuple[ReviewDecision, int, str]:\n1142:         \"\"\"Parse OpenCode output with controlled retry for transient parse failures.\n1143: \n1144:         Returns (decision, parse_attempts, parse_method).\n1145: \n1146:         Before: A parse failure could immediately lead to INSPECT and potential\n1147:                 HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n1148:         During: When parser returns INSPECT with fallback_inspect but output\n1149:                 appears valid (non-empty stdout, no technical errors), retry\n1150:                 parsing up to max_retries times.\n1151:         After: Returns (decision, parse_attempts, parse_method) for audit trail.\n1152:         \"\"\"\n1153:         # First attempt\n1154:         decision, parse_method = self._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n1155:         parse_attempts = 1\n1156: \n1157:         # If fallback INSPECT and output looks valid (not a technical failure), retry\n1158:         if decision == ReviewDecision.INSPECT and parse_method == \"fallback_inspect\":\n1159:             is_technical_failure = (\n1160:                 \"TimeoutExpired\" in stderr\n1161:                 or \"FileNotFoundError\" in stderr\n1162:                 or \"OSError\" in stderr\n1163:                 or not stdout.strip()\n1164:             )\n1165:             if not is_technical_failure and stdout.strip():\n1166:                 for retry in range(max_retries):\n1167:                     time.sleep(0.1 * (2**retry))\n1168:                     decision, parse_method = self._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n1169:                     parse_attempts += 1\n1170:                     if parse_method != \"fallback_inspect\":\n1171:                         break\n1172: \n1173:         return decision, parse_attempts, parse_method\n1174: \n1175:     def _parse_opencode_decision(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[ReviewDecision, str]:\n1176:         \"\"\"Parse OpenCode output for DECISION: APPROVE|CHANGES|INSPECT pattern.\n1177: \n1178:         Returns (decision, parse_method):\n1179:           parse_method is one of:\n1180:             - \"json_final_answer\"  — NDJSON final_answer phase\n1181:             - \"json_last_text\"     — NDJSON last text event\n1182:             - \"text_regex\"         — DECISION: pattern via regex\n1183:             - \"explicit_inspect\"   — DECISION: INSPECT explicitly found\n1184:             - \"fallback_inspect\"   — parser default, no pattern recognized\n1185: \n1186:         Prioridad de parseo:\n1187:         1. Si hay formato JSON (NDJSON), usar el parser estructurado.\n1188:         2. Buscar patron estructurado DECISION:\\\\s*(APPROVE|CHANGES|INSPECT).\n1189:         3. NO hay fallback a palabra desnuda — si no hay patron estructurado,\n1190:            retorna INSPECT + \"fallback_inspect\" para evitar falsos positivos.\n1191:         \"\"\"\n1192:         if self._supports_json_format:\n1193:             json_decision, json_method = self._parse_opencode_json_decision(stdout)\n1194:             if json_decision != ReviewDecision.INSPECT:\n1195:                 return json_decision, json_method\n1196: \n1197:         # Look for explicit DECISION: pattern only (no bare word fallback)\n1198:         stdout_upper = stdout.upper()\n1199: \n1200:         if re.search(r\"DECISION:\\s*CHANGES\", stdout_upper):\n1201:             return ReviewDecision.CHANGES, \"text_regex\"\n1202:         if re.search(r\"DECISION:\\s*APPROVE\", stdout_upper):\n1203:             return ReviewDecision.APPROVE, \"text_regex\"\n1204:         if re.search(r\"DECISION:\\s*INSPECT\", stdout_upper):\n1205:             return ReviewDecision.INSPECT, \"explicit_inspect\"\n1206: \n1207:         return ReviewDecision.INSPECT, \"fallback_inspect\"\n1208: \n1209:     def run_manager_review_cycle(  # noqa: C901\n1210:         self,\n1211:         *,\n1212:         ticket_id: str,\n1213:         supervisor,\n1214:         manager_executable: Path | None = None,\n1215:         timeout_seconds: int | None = None,\n1216:     ) -> ReviewResult:\n1217:         \"\"\"Run manager review cycle with structured CHANGES validation and HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n1218: \n1219:         Before: Simple review loop with max_attempts retry, no structure validation.\n1220:         During: Persists each attempt to attempt-N.md, validates CHANGES structure,\n1221:                 tracks consecutive CHANGES decisions, escalates to HUMAN_GATE at 5th rejection.\n1222:         After: Returns ReviewResult; bus contains lightweight review_log_path + stdout_tail;\n1223:                human_review_report.md generated if threshold reached.\n1224: \n1225:         WP-2026-118: Fail-safe added - if emit() fails critically, abort cycle with\n1226:         auditable error instead of crashing with raw traceback.\n1227:         \"\"\"\n1228:         latest_state = self.state_ingest._latest_state(ticket_id)\n1229:         if latest_state != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\":\n1230:             return ReviewResult(\n1231:                 decision=ReviewDecision.INSPECT,\n1232:                 stdout=\"\",\n1233:                 stderr=f\"Review requested but ticket state is {latest_state}, expected READY_FOR_REVIEW.\",\n1234:                 exit_code=1,\n1235:                 feedback=\"Review bridge blocked due to invalid ticket state.\",\n1236:             )\n1237: \n1238:         # WP-2026-118: Wrap initial emit in fail-safe\n1239:         try:\n1240:             self.event_bus.emit(\n1241:                 \"MANAGER_REVIEWING\",\n1242:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1243:                 actor=\"MANAGER\",\n1244:                 payload={\"state\": latest_state},\n1245:             )\n1246:         except Exception as exc:\n1247:             # WP-2026-118: Fail-safe - abort cycle cleanly if bus fails at startup\n1248:             error_msg = (\n1249:                 f\"[manager-review-bridge] FAIL-SAFE: Cannot emit MANAGER_REVIEWING for \"\n1250:                 f\"ticket {ticket_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}. \"\n1251:                 f\"Review cycle aborted to prevent ambiguous state.\"\n1252:             )\n1253:             print(error_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n1254:             return ReviewResult(\n1255:                 decision=ReviewDecision.INSPECT,\n1256:                 stdout=\"\",\n1257:                 stderr=error_msg,\n1258:                 exit_code=1,\n1259:                 feedback=\"Review bridge aborted due to event_bus failure at startup.\",\n1260:             )\n1261: \n1262:         cfg = self._load_review_config()\n1263:         # Honor agents.json unless a non-default (non-300) timeout is provided\n1264:         if timeout_seconds is None or timeout_seconds == 300:\n1265:             base_timeout = cfg[\"timeout_seconds\"]\n1266:         else:\n1267:             base_timeout = timeout_seconds\n1268: \n1269:         max_attempts = cfg[\"max_attempts\"]\n1270:         multiplier = cfg[\"retry_backoff_multiplier\"]\n1271: \n1272:         dtype = self.state_ingest._read_deliverable_type()\n1273:         prompt = self._build_review_prompt(ticket_id, dtype)\n1274: \n1275:         # Dispatch based on backend assigned to MANAGER role\n1276:         backend = self._get_manager_backend()\n1277: \n1278:         final_stdout, final_stderr, final_exit = \"\", \"\", 0\n1279:         decision = ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n1280:         parse_method = \"\"\n1281:         transport_ok = True\n1282:         transport_error = \"\"\n1283: \n1284:         # WP-2026-106 B3: the escalation counter is derived purely from bus\n1285:         # history, never a local loop accumulator. It is computed once, after\n1286:         # this cycle emits its REVIEW_DECISION, so a bridge restart or an\n1287:         # internal retry can neither inflate nor reset it.\n1288:         review_attempts_payloads: list[dict] = []\n1289: \n1290:         for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):\n1291:             current_timeout = int(base_timeout * (multiplier ** (attempt - 1)))\n1292:             start_time = time.time()\n1293: \n1294:             if backend == \"opencode\":\n1295:                 # OpenCode route\n1296:                 stdout, stderr, exit_code = self._run_opencode_review(\n1297:                     ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1298:                     prompt=prompt,\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-1298. Use offset=1299 to continue.)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# ruff: noqa: S603\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom .event_bus import EventBus\nfrom .skill_resolver import SkillResolver, create_resolver\nfrom .utils import count_trailing_changes\n\n","truncated":true,"loaded":[]},"title":"bus\\review_bridge.py","time":{"start":1779709716101,"end":1779709716128}},"id":"prt_e5ef71fdc001hTz2UqKIsfh6CX","sessionID":"ses_1a1094f1effeORIA49p5RbFkci","messageID":"msg_e5ef70e67001cQO1DmJXa2GYjN"}}
