\".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n880:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n881: \n882:         # Create minimal work_plan.md\n883:         (collaboration_dir / \"work_plan.md\").write_text(\n884:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n### Metadata\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-102\\n- **deliverable_type:** code\\n\",\n885:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n886:         )\n887: \n888:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n889:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n890:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n891: \n892:         # Emit current state directly to bus (no snapshot files)\n893:         event_bus.emit(\n894:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n895:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n896:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n897:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n898:         )\n899: \n900:         # Mock the review execution to capture that it was called\n901:         captured = {}\n902: \n903:         def fake_opencode_review(*, ticket_id, prompt, attempt=1, manager_executable=None, timeout_seconds):\n904:             captured[\"prompt_includes_ticket\"] = \"WP-2026-102\" in prompt\n905:             return \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\", 0\n906: \n907:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n908:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_run_opencode_review\", fake_opencode_review)\n909: \n910:         class DummySupervisor:\n911:             def transition_ticket(self, ticket_id, new_state, reason):\n912:                 captured[\"transition\"] = (ticket_id, new_state, reason)\n913: \n914:         result = bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n915:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n916:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n917:             timeout_seconds=5,\n918:         )\n919: \n920:         assert result.decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n921:         assert captured.get(\"prompt_includes_ticket\") is True\n922:         assert captured.get(\"transition\") is not None\n923: \n924: \n925: class TestSupervisorNonTerminalStates:\n926:     \"\"\"Tests for supervisor NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant (WP-2026-102).\"\"\"\n927: \n928:     def test_non_terminal_states_constant_exists(self):\n929:         \"\"\"Test NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant is defined.\"\"\"\n930:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n931: \n932:         assert NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not None\n933:         assert isinstance(NON_TERMINAL_STATES, frozenset)\n934: \n935:     def test_is_state_terminal_method(self, tmp_path):\n936:         \"\"\"Test _is_state_terminal correctly identifies terminal vs non-terminal states.\"\"\"\n937:         from bus.state_machine import TicketState\n938:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n939: \n940:         # Non-terminal states should return False\n941:         for state in NON_TERMINAL_STATES:\n942:             assert state not in set(), \"Sanity check: NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not empty\"\n943: \n944:         # COMPLETED and UNKNOWN are terminal\n945:         assert TicketState.COMPLETED not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n946:         assert TicketState.UNKNOWN not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n947: \n948:     def test_supervisor_bootstrap_preserves_active_ticket_in_non_terminal_state(self, tmp_path):\n949:         \"\"\"Test bootstrap does NOT clear active_ticket when bus is in non-terminal state.\"\"\"\n950:         from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor, SupervisorState\n951: \n952:         collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n953:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n954:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n955:         runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n956: \n957:         # Create execution_log showing COMPLETED (but bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW)\n958:         (collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\").write_text(\n959:             \"# Execution Log\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n**Estado:** COMPLETED\\n\",\n960:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n961:         )\n962: \n963:         supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n964:             project_root=tmp_path,\n965:             collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n966:             runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n967:             auto_sync=False,\n968:         )\n969: \n970:         # Set up state with active ticket\n971:         supervisor.save_state(\n972:             SupervisorState(active_ticket=\"WP-2026-102\", completed_tickets=[])\n973:         )\n974: \n975:         # Bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal) - this takes precedence over execution_log\n976:         supervisor.event_bus.emit(\n977:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n978:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n979:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n980:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n981:         )\n982: \n983:         supervisor.bootstrap()\n984: \n985:         # active_ticket should be preserved because bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal)\n986:         # The bootstrap logic only clears active_ticket when:\n987:         # 1. execution_log_status == \"COMPLETED\" AND\n988:         # 2. current_state (from bus) is terminal\n989:         # Since current_state is READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal), active_ticket is preserved\n990:         state = supervisor.load_state()\n991:         assert state.active_ticket == \"WP-2026-102\"\n992: \n993: \n994: # {"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1779709927790,"sessionID":"ses_1a1070c43ffeMtFLCiaGdQ7E2Y","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"toolu_a479470136b248a895cde229","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md"},"output":"<path>C:\\Users\\fdl\\Proyectos_Python\\z_scripts\\orquestador_de_agentes\\.agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Work Plan - WP-2026-134\n2: \n3: ## Metadata\n4: - **ID:** WP-2026-134\n5: - **Estado:** APPROVED\n6: - **deliverable_type:** code\n7: - **Titulo:** Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n8: - **Asignado a:** Builder\n9: \n10: ## Objetivo\n11: El Manager no debe revisar el working tree vacio tras el commit del Builder. Este ticket ancla el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añade provenance del ultimo commit y mantiene el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n12: \n13: ## Decision Arquitectonica\n14: - `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n15: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n16: - Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n17: - `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.\n18: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` del prompt declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory y no sustituye el juicio del Manager.\n19: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n20: - No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.\n21: \n22: ## Files Likely Touched\n23: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n24: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n25: \n26: ## Fases\n27: 1. Cambiar el origen del diff a `origin/main...HEAD` en `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()`, con fallback a `git diff HEAD` si el remote base no es alcanzable.\n28: 2. Implementar `_git_provenance()` y añadir la seccion correspondiente al prompt del Manager.\n29: 3. Actualizar el bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` para dejar claro que la revision es advisory.\n30: 4. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n31: \n32: ## Calidad\n33: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n34: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n35: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n36: \n37: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n38: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n39: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n40: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n41: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n42: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n43: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n44: \n45: ## Nota\n46: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. El schema estructurado y el panel multi-reviewer quedan para un ticket posterior si hace falta.\n\n(End of file - total 46 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Work Plan - WP-2026-134\n\n## Metadata\n- **ID:** WP-2026-134\n- **Estado:** APPROVED\n- **deliverable_type:** code\n- **Titulo:** Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n- **Asignado a:** Builder\n\n## Objetivo\nEl Manager no debe revisar el working tree vacio tras el commit del Builder. Este ticket ancla el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añade provenance del ultimo commit y mantiene el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n- Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n- Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n- `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.\n- El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` del prompt declara de forma explicita que la revision es advisory y no sustituye el juicio del Manager.\n- El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` permanece intacto.\n- No se introduce schema JSON estructurado ni panel multi-reviewer en este ticket.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\collaboration\\work_plan.md","time":{"start":1779709927768,"end":1779709927788}},"id":"prt_e5efa5e01001bCluf7L5xAPlfa","sessionID":"ses_1a1070c43ffeMtFLCiaGdQ7E2Y","messageID":"msg_e5efa3d33001HQuZmu95hH4UYN"}}

Esto es consistente con la estructura existente del proyecto y no afecta la funcionalidad.\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Ajustar tests para verificar el nuevo diff base, el fallback, la seccion de provenance y el contrato de salida.\n139: \n140: ## Files Likely Touched\n141: - `bus/review_bridge.py`\n142: - `tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py`\n143: \n144: ## Calidad\n145: - `ruff check . && ruff format --check .`\n146: - `python scripts/run_pytest_safe.py tests/unit/test_manager_review_bridge.py -q`\n147: - `python .agent/agent_controller.py --validate --json --force`\n148: \n149: ## Criterios de aceptacion\n150: - El review prompt incluye `git diff origin/main...HEAD` cuando `origin/main` esta disponible.\n151: - Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable, el prompt degrada a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n152: - El prompt incluye una seccion `--- git provenance ---` con SHA, fecha y autor.\n153: - El bloque `INSTRUCTIONS` deja explicito que la revision es advisory.\n154: - El contrato `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` sigue funcionando sin cambios.\n155: - Los tests reflejan el nuevo contexto de review y siguen pasando.\n156: \n157: ### Casos de test esperados\n158: - `test_build_review_prompt_uses_branch_base_diff`\n159: - `test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote`\n160: - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_provenance_section`\n161: - Los tests existentes de composicion del prompt siguen pasando:\n162:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_generated_artifacts_block`\n163:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_allowed_skills_for_role`\n164: \n165: ## Nota\n166: Inspirado en el patron de OpenClaw `autoreview`: bundle anclado a branch base, provenance y contrato advisory. El schema estructurado y el panel multi-reviewer quedan para un ticket posterior si hace falta.\n167: \n168: \n169: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-134.md ---\n170: # AUDIT_WP-2026-134 - Anchor Manager review diff to branch base and add provenance\n171: \n172: ## Verificacion inicial\n173: El review bridge ya empaqueta contexto canonical, pero hoy su diff sale de `git diff HEAD`, que puede quedar vacio una vez el Builder commitea. El gap real es de anclaje de diff y trazabilidad, no de state machine.\n174: \n175: ## Hallazgos\n176: - `bus/review_bridge.py` construye el diff con `git diff HEAD` y el diff-stat con el mismo anclaje al working tree.\n177: - El prompt del Manager ya contiene un bloque de instrucciones, pero no declara de forma explicita que la review es advisory.\n178: - No existe una seccion compacta de provenance del commit en el prompt actual.\n179: - El ticket necesita cubrir el caso en que `origin/main` no sea alcanzable o no exista en el entorno, degradando a `git diff HEAD` con warning visible.\n180: - Los tests del bridge tendran que validar el nuevo origen del diff, el fallback y la presencia de provenance sin cambiar `DECISION`.\n181: \n182: ## Riesgo observado\n183: Si el ticket intenta meter schema JSON estructurado o panel multi-reviewer, el alcance se amplifica y se aleja del objetivo principal: revisar el diff real del ticket con trazabilidad.\n184: \n185: ## Verificacion esperada\n186: - El Manager recibe un diff anclado a `origin/main...HEAD` cuando el remote base esta disponible.\n187: - Si `origin/main` no es accesible, el prompt muestra un warning y degrada a `git diff HEAD`.\n188: - El prompt incorpora provenance del ultimo commit del ticket.\n189: - La instruccion advisory queda explicita.\n190: - La salida canonica del review sigue siendo `DECISION: APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT`.\n191: \n192: ## Casos de test esperados\n193: - `test_build_review_prompt_uses_branch_base_diff`\n194: - `test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote`\n195: - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_provenance_section`\n196: - Mantener verdes:\n197:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_generated_artifacts_block`\n198:   - `test_build_review_prompt_includes_allowed_skills_for_role`\n199: \n200: \n201: --- git provenance ---\n202: [WARNING: origin/main not reachable] bd4034c6ccad23c5af2dfd60bcc0a459f48f6c15 2026-05-25 13:16:53 +0200 fdl\n203: \n204: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n205: Available skills for this role:\n206:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n207:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n208:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n209: \n210: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n211: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n212: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n213: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n214: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n215: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n216: - .session_state.json\n217: \n218: \n219: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n220: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n221: \n222: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n223: \n224: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n225: DECISION: APPROVE\n226: \n227: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n228: \n229: ## SUMMARY\n230: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n231: \n232: ## BLOCKERS\n233: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n234: \n235: ## SUGGESTIONS\n236: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n237: \n238: DECISION: CHANGES\n239: \n240: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n241: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 241 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-134. Este ticket ancla el contexto de review al diff real del ticket, añade provenance del ultimo commit y mantiene el contrato canonico de decisiones intacto.\n\n## Decision Arquitectonica\n- `_git_diff_stat()` y `_build_diff_for_files_likely_touched()` usan `origin/main...HEAD` como base preferida.\n- Si `origin/main` no es alcanzable o no existe en el entorno, el bridge degrada de forma segura a `git diff HEAD` y muestra un warning explicito en el prompt del Manager.\n- Se añade `_git_provenance()` para emitir una linea compacta con SHA, fecha y autor del ultimo commit relevante del ticket.\n- `_build_review_prompt()` inserta una seccion `--- git provenance ---` antes del diff para dar trazabilidad al Manager.","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":".agent\\runtime\\review_packets\\WP-2026-134_attempt-1.md","time":{"start":1779711806627,"end":1779711806703}},"id":"prt_e5f1700e50013rUqLj0QAXkevx","sessionID":"ses_1a0e9c1e0ffeun1p9ZEFJBb22O","messageID":"msg_e5f163f0f001xfqgTxRI20LGz1"}}

transport.\n53:     \"\"\"\n54: \n55:     ticket_id: str\n56:     state: str\n57:     deliverable_type: str\n58: \n59: \n60: class TicketStateIngest:\n61:     \"\"\"Canonical ticket state ingestion \n126:     def get_ticket_context(self, ticket_id: str | None = None) -> TicketContext | None:\n127:         \"\"\"Get a canonical TicketContext for review transport.\n128: \n129:         Before: Requires ticket_id or falls back to work_plan.md active ticket.\n130:         During: Queries event_bus for state, work_plan.md for deliverable_type.\n131:         After: Returns TicketContext or None TicketStateIngest for state, builds prompts, executes review.\n151:     After: Returns ReviewResult with decision and emits events to bus.\n152:     \"\"\"\n153: \n154:     def __init__(\n155: capture_output=True,\n353:                 text=True,\n354:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n355:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n356:                 timeout=10,\n357:             )\n358:             return \"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable, using HEAD fallback]\\n\" + (\n359:                 result.stdout or \"[git diff --stat empty]\"\n360:             )\n361:         except Exception as e:\n362:             return f\"[Error fetching git diff --stat: {e}]\"\n363: \n364:     def _build_diff_for_files_likely_touched(\n365:         self, ticket_id: str, budget_bytes: int\n366:     ) -> str:\n367:         try:\n368:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n369:             result = subprocess.run(\n370:                 [git_bin, \"diff\", \"origin/main...HEAD\"],\n371:                 capture_output=True,\n372:                 text=True,\n373:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n374:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n375:                 timeout=15,\n376:             )\n377:             warning_prefix = \"\"\n378:             if result.returncode != 0:\n379:                 result = subprocess.run(\n380:                     [git_bin, \"diff\", \"HEAD\"],\n381:                     capture_output=True,\n382:                     text=True,\n383:                     encoding=\"utf-8\",\n384:                     cwd=self.project_root,\n385:                     timeout=15,\n386:                 )\n387:                 warning_prefix = \"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable, using git diff HEAD fallback]\\n\"\n388:             diff = warning_prefix + (result.stdout or \"\")\n389:             diff_bytes = diff.encode(\"utf-8\")\n390:             if len(diff_bytes) <= budget_bytes:\n391:                 return diff\n392:             truncated = diff_bytes[:budget_bytes].decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\")\n393:             return truncated + \"\\n\\n[diff truncado por budget]\"\n394:         except Exception as e:\n395:             return f\"[Error fetching git diff: {e}]\"\n396: \n397:     def _git_provenance(self) -> str:\n398:         try:\n399:             git_bin = shutil.which(\"git\") or \"git\"\n400:             result = subprocess.run(\n401:                 [\n402:                     git_bin,\n403:                     \"log\",\n404:                     \"origin/main..HEAD\",\n405:                     \"--format=%H %ai %an\",\n406:                     \"--no-merges\",\n407:                     \"-1\",\n408:                 ],\n409:                 capture_output=True,\n410:                 text=True,\n411:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n412:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n413:                 timeout=10,\n414:             )\n415:             if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():\n416:                 return result.stdout.strip()\n417:             result = subprocess.run(\n418:                 [git_bin, \"log\", \"HEAD\", \"--format=%H %ai %an\", \"--no-merges\", \"-1\"],\n419:                 capture_output=True,\n420:                 text=True,\n421:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n422:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n423:                 timeout=10,\n424:             )\n425:             line = result.stdout.strip() or \"[no commits found]\"\n426:             return f\"[WARNING: origin/main not reachable] {line}\"\n427:         except Exception as e:\n428:             return f\"[Error fetching git provenance: {e}]\"\n429: \n430:     def _rubric_for_type(self, dtype: str, ticket_id: str) -> str:\n431:         if dtype == \"code\":\n432:             return (\n433:                 f\"Review code ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n434:                 f\"Verify the implementation correctness, testing coverage, and style guides. \"\n435:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n436:             )\n437:         elif dtype == \"mixed\":\n438:             return (\n439:                 f\"Review mixed ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n440:                 f\"Verify code correctness, tests, and style guides, and additionally verify \"\n441:                 f\"that all declared non-code deliverables exist, are well-structured, and are fully complete. \"\n442:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n443:             )\n444:         else:  # documentation, research, analysis\n445:             return (\n446:                 f\"Review non-code {dtype} ticket {ticket_id}. \"\n447:                 f\"Since this is a non-code deliverable, focus strictly on the clarity, depth, correctness, \"\n448:                 f\"structure, and completeness of the requested document deliverables. \"\n449:                 f\"Check acceptance criteria and Files Likely Touched.\"\n450:             )\n451: \n452:     def _build_review_prompt(self, ticket_id: str, dtype: str) -> str:\n453:         # P1-2: work_plan, STATE, TURN (intocables)\n454:         sections = [\n455:             (name, self.state_ingest._read_canonical(name))\n456:             for name in (\"work_plan.md\", \"STATE.md\", \"TURN.md\")\n457:         ]\n458: \n459:         # P3: execution_log section\n460:         sections.append(\n461:             (\n462:                 \"execution_log.md (ticket section)\",\n463:                 self._extract_ticket_section(ticket_id),\n464:             )\n465:         )\n466: \n467:         # P4: PLAN + AUDIT opcionales\n468:         for name in (f\"PLAN_{ticket_id}.md\", f\"AUDIT_{ticket_id}.md\"):\n469:             content = self.state_ingest._read_canonical_optional(name)\n470:             if content is not None:\n471:                 sections.append((name, content))\n472: \n473:         # Calculate used bytes\n474:         used = sum(len(c.encode(\"utf-8\")) for _, c in sections)\n475: \n476:         hard_cap_bytes = 80 * 1024\n477:         canonical_budget = 60 * 1024\n478: \n479:         # P4.5: git provenance (compact, inserted before diff)\n480:         provenance = self._git_provenance()\n481:         sections.append((\"git provenance\", provenance))\n482:         used += len(provenance.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n483: \n484:         # P5: diff con budget restante\n485:         if used < canonical_budget:\n486:             remaining = hard_cap_bytes - used\n487:             diff = self._build_diff_for_files_likely_touched(ticket_id, remaining)\n488:             if diff:\n489:                 sections.append((\"git diff (Files Likely Touched)\", diff))\n490:         else:\n491:             sections.append(\n492:                 (\n493:                     \"git diff --stat\",\n494:                     self._git_diff_stat()\n495:                     + \"\\n[diff omitido por budget. Read files directly if needed.]\",\n496:                 )\n497:             )\n498: \n499:         # Compose\n500:         parts = [self._rubric_for_type(dtype, ticket_id)]\n501:         for name, content in sections:\n502:             parts.append(f\"\\n--- {name} ---\\n{content}\")\n503: \n504:         # WP-2026-127: Add skill filtering by role\n505:         current_role = self._get_current_role()\n506:         allowed_skills = self.skill_resolver.filter_skills_for_prompt(\n507:             current_role, include_metadata=False\n508:         )\n509:         parts.append(\n510:             f\"\\n--- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE {current_role} ---\\n{allowed_skills}\"\n511:         )\n512: \n513:         parts.append(\n514:             \"\\n--- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\\n\"\n515:             \"Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\\n\"\n516:             \"Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\\n\"\n517:             \"- PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\\n\"\n518:             \"- review_queue.md, notifications.md\\n\"\n519:             \"- archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\\n\"\n520:             \"- .session_state.json\\n\"\n521:         )\n522:         parts.append(\n523:             \"\\n--- INSTRUCTIONS ---\\n\"\n524:             \"This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\\n\\n\"\n525:             \"Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion \"\n526:             \"are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed \"\n527:             \"by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\\n\\n\"\n528:             \"If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\\n\"\n529:             \"DECISION: APPROVE\\n\\n\"\n530:             \"If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure \"\n531:             \"(headings included) before the final line:\\n\\n\"\n532:             \"## SUMMARY\\n\"\n533:             \"<one line: why the ticket is not approved>\\n\\n\"\n534:             \"## BLOCKERS\\n\"\n535:             \"- <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\\n\\n\"\n536:             \"## SUGGESTIONS\\n\"\n537:             \"- <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\\n\\n\"\n538:             \"DECISION: CHANGES\\n\\n\"\n539:             \"If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\\n\"\n540:             \"DECISION: INSPECT\\n\"\n541:         )\n542:         return \"\\n\".join(parts)\n543: \n544:     def _run_legacy_manager_review(\n545:         self,\n546:         *,\n547:         ticket_id: str,\n548:         manager_executable: Path,\n549:         timeout_seconds: int,\n550:     ) -> tuple[str, str, int]:\n551:         \"\"\"Legacy Manager review route. Preserved for backward compatibility.\"\"\"\n552:         exe_str = str(manager_executable)\n553:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_str.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n554:             cmd_candidate = Path(exe_str).with_suffix(\".cmd\")\n555:             bat_candidate = Path(exe_str).with_suffix(\".bat\")\n556:             if cmd_candidate.exists():\n557:                 exe_str = str(cmd_candidate)\n558:             elif bat_candidate.exists():\n559:                 exe_str = str(bat_candidate)\n560: \n561:         command = [exe_str, \"review\", ticket_id]\n562:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_str.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n563:             command = [\n564:                 \"powershell.exe\",\n565:                 \"-NoProfile\",\n566:                 \"-ExecutionPolicy\",\n567:                 \"Bypass\",\n568:                 \"-File\",\n569:                 *command,\n570:             ]\n571: \n572:         result = subprocess.run(\n573:             command,\n574:             capture_output=True,\n575:             text=True,\n576:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n577:             cwd=self.project_root,\n578:             env=self._review_env(),\n579:             timeout=timeout_seconds,\n580:         )\n581:         return result.stdout or \"\", result.stderr or \"\", result.returncode\n582: \n583:     def _run_opencode_review(  # noqa: C901\n584:         self,\n585:         *,\n586:         ticket_id: str,\n587:         prompt: str,\n588:         attempt: int = 1,\n589:         manager_executable: Path | None = None,\n590:         timeout_seconds: int,\n591:     ) -> tuple[str, str, int]:\n592:         \"\"\"OpenCode review route using manager agent spec with context prompt.\n593: \n594:         Transporte: el contexto completo se escribe a un review packet en\n595:         .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md y el prompt\n596:         posicional es corto, indicando al Manager que lo lea. Evita el flag\n597:         --file (array que consume el mensaje) y el limite de longitud de la\n598:         CLI.\n599:         \"\"\"\n600:         model = self._get_manager_model()\n601: \n602:         executable = str(manager_executable) if manager_executable else \"opencode\"\n603:         if os.name == \"nt\" and executable == \"opencode\":\n604:             executable = \"opencode.cmd\"\n605: \n606:         exe_full = shutil.which(executable) or executable\n607:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_full.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n608:             cmd_candidate = Path(exe_full).with_suffix(\".cmd\")\n609:             bat_candidate = Path(exe_full).with_suffix(\".bat\")\n610:             if cmd_candidate.exists():\n611:                 exe_full = str(cmd_candidate)\n612:             elif bat_candidate.exists():\n613:                 exe_full = str(bat_candidate)\n614: \n615:         # Review packet: el contexto completo del review se escribe a un\n616:         # archivo dentro del repo; el Manager lo lee con sus herramientas.\n617:         # El prompt posicional es corto (sin metacaracteres de cmd.exe).\n618:         packet_path = self._get_review_packet_path(ticket_id, attempt)\n619:         packet_path.write_text(prompt, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n620:         packet_rel = packet_path.relative_to(self.project_root).as_posix()\n621: \n622:         review_message = (\n623:             f\"Revisa {ticket_id} leyendo el archivo {packet_rel} con tus \"\n624:             \"herramientas de lectura. Termina con exactamente \"\n625:             \"DECISION: APPROVE o DECISION: CHANGES.\"\n626:         )\n627: \n628:         if not review_message.isascii():\n629:             raise ValueError(\n630:                 \"Review message must be ASCII for Windows command transport.\"\n631:             )\n632: \n633:         cmd_args = [exe_full, \"run\", \"--agent\", \"manager\"]\n634:         if model:\n635:             cmd_args.extend([\"--model\", model])\n636:         if self._supports_json_format:\n637:             cmd_args.extend([\"--format\", \"json\"])\n638:         cmd_args.append(review_message)\n639: \n640:         use_shell = False\n641:         if os.name == \"nt\" and (\n642:             exe_full.lower().endswith(\".cmd\")\n643:             or exe_full.lower().endswith(\".bat\")\n644:             or \"opencode\" in exe_full.lower()\n645:         ):\n646:             use_shell = True\n647: \n648:         if os.name == \"nt\" and exe_full.lower().endswith(\".ps1\"):\n649:             cmd_args = [\n650:                 \"powershell.exe\",\n651:                 \"-NoProfile\",\n652:                 \"-ExecutionPolicy\",\n653:                 \"Bypass\",\n654:                 \"-File\",\n655:                 *cmd_args,\n656:             ]\n657: \n658:         try:\n659:             # En Windows, shell=True con una lista hace que cmd.exe trate\n660:             # args[1:] como argumentos del propio shell: opencode arrancaria\n661:             # sin ninguno. list2cmdline produce el string correcto.\n662:             run_args = subprocess.list2cmdline(cmd_args) if use_shell else cmd_args\n663:             result = subprocess.run(\n664:                 run_args,\n665:                 capture_output=True,\n666:                 text=True,\n667:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n668:                 cwd=self.project_root,\n669:                 timeout=timeout_seconds,\n670:                 shell=use_shell,\n671:             )\n672:         except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError, OSError) as exc:\n673:             err_msg = f\"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}\"\n674:             if isinstance(exc, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):\n675:                 err_msg = f\"TimeoutExpired: {exc}\"\n676:             return \"\", err_msg, 1\n677: \n678:         return result.stdout or \"\", result.stderr or \"\", result.returncode\n679: \n680:     def _load_review_config(self) -> dict:\n681:         \"\"\"Load review configuration from agents.json with WP-2026-106 defaults.\n682: \n683:         Before: max_attempts was 2 (legacy default).\n684:         During: Reads agents.json manager_review section, applies WP-2026-106 threshold of 5.\n685:         After: Returns config dict with max_attempts=5 for HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n686:         \"\"\"\n687:         config_path = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"config\" / \"agents.json\"\n688:         defaults = {\n689:             \"timeout_seconds\": 180,\n690:             \"max_attempts\": 5,  # WP-2026-106: elevated from 3 to 5 for HUMAN_GATE\n691:             \"retry_backoff_multiplier\": 2.0,\n692:         }\n693:         if not config_path.exists():\n694:             return defaults\n695:         try:\n696:             data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n697:             cfg = data.get(\"manager_review\", {})\n698:             return {\n699:                 \"timeout_seconds\": int(\n700:                     cfg.get(\"timeout_seconds\", defaults[\"timeout_seconds\"])\n701:                 ),\n702:                 \"max_attempts\": int(cfg.get(\"max_attempts\", defaults[\"max_attempts\"])),\n703:                 \"retry_backoff_multiplier\": float(\n704:                     cfg.get(\n705:                         \"retry_backoff_multiplier\", defaults[\"retry_backoff_multiplier\"]\n706:                     )\n707:                 ),\n708:             }\n709:         except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, TypeError):\n710:             return defaults\n711: \n712:     def _get_review_log_path(self, ticket_id: str) -> Path:\n713:         \"\"\"Get the review log directory for a ticket.\n714: \n715:         Before: Requires ticket_id string.\n716:         During: Creates directory structure under .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/.\n717:         After: Returns Path object for the ticket's review log directory.\n718:         \"\"\"\n719:         reviews_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"reviews\"\n720:         ticket_dir = reviews_dir / ticket_id\n721:         ticket_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n722:         return ticket_dir\n723: \n724:     def _get_review_packet_path(self, ticket_id: str, attempt: int) -> Path:\n725:         \"\"\"Get the canonical review packet path for a ticket attempt.\"\"\"\n726:         packets_dir = self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"review_packets\"\n727:         packets_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n728:         return packets_dir / f\"{ticket_id}_attempt-{attempt}.md\"\n729: \n730:     def _persist_review_attempt(\n731:         self,\n732:         ticket_id: str,\n733:         attempt: int,\n734:         stdout: str,\n735:         stderr: str,\n736:         decision: ReviewDecision,\n737:         review_packet_path: Path | None = None,\n738:         parse_method: str = \"\",\n739:         transport_ok: bool = True,\n740:         transport_error: str = \"\",\n741:     ) -> Path:\n742:         \"\"\"Persist a review attempt to attempt-N.md idempotently.\n743: \n744:         Before: Requires ticket_id, attempt number, stdout, stderr, and decision.\n745:         During: Writes review content to .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/attempt-N.md,\n746:                 overwriting any existing file with the same attempt number.\n747:         After: Returns the Path to the persisted review file.\n748:         \"\"\"\n749:         ticket_dir = self._get_review_log_path(ticket_id)\n750:         attempt_file = ticket_dir / f\"attempt-{attempt}.md\"\n751: \n752:         # Build structured review content\n753:         content_parts = [\n754:             f\"# Review Attempt {attempt}\",\n755:             \"\",\n756:             f\"## Ticket: {ticket_id}\",\n757:             f\"## Decision: {decision.value.upper()}\",\n758:             f\"## Parse Method: {parse_method or 'unknown'}\",\n759:             f\"## Transport OK: {transport_ok}\",\n760:             \"\",\n761:             \"## Review Packet\",\n762:             \"\",\n763:             str(review_packet_path.relative_to(self.project_root).as_posix())\n764:             if review_packet_path is not None\n765:             else \"[not recorded]\",\n766:             \"\",\n767:             \"## Transport Error\",\n768:             \"\",\n769:             transport_error or \"[none]\",\n770:             \"\",\n771:             \"## STDOUT\",\n772:             \"\",\n773:             stdout or \"[empty]\",\n774:             \"\",\n775:             \"## STDERR\",\n776:             \"\",\n777:             stderr or \"[empty]\",\n778:             \"\",\n779:         ]\n780: \n781:         # Parse and add structured sections for CHANGES decisions\n782:         if decision == ReviewDecision.CHANGES:\n783:             structured = self._parse_changes_structure(stdout)\n784:             content_parts.extend(\n785:                 [\n786:                     \"\",\n787:                     \"## SUMMARY\",\n788:                     \"\",\n789:                     structured.get(\"summary\", \"[no summary provided]\"),\n790:                     \"\",\n791:                     \"## BLOCKERS\",\n792:                     \"\",\n793:                     structured.get(\"blockers\", \"[no blockers provided]\"),\n794:                     \"\",\n795:                     \"## SUGGESTIONS\",\n796:                     \"\",\n797:                     structured.get(\"suggestions\", \"[no suggestions provided]\"),\n798:                     \"\",\n799:                 ]\n800:             )\n801: \n802:         attempt_file.write_text(\"\\n\".join(content_parts), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n803:         return attempt_file\n804: \n805:     def _parse_changes_structure(self, stdout: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n806:         \"\"\"Parse structured sections from CHANGES review output.\n807: \n808:         Before: Requires stdout string from Manager review.\n809:         During: Extracts SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS sections using regex patterns.\n810:         After: Returns dict with 'summary', 'blockers', 'suggestions' keys (defaults if not found).\n811:         \"\"\"\n812:         result = {\"summary\": \"\", \"blockers\": \"\", \"suggestions\": \"\"}\n813: \n814:         # Pattern for ## SUMMARY section\n815:         summary_match = re.search(\n816:             r\"##\\s*SUMMARY\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:BLOCKERS|SUGGESTIONS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n817:             stdout,\n818:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n819:         )\n820:         if summary_match:\n821:             result[\"summary\"] = summary_match.group(1).strip()\n822: \n823:         # Pattern for ## BLOCKERS section\n824:         blockers_match = re.search(\n825:             r\"##\\s*BLOCKERS\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:SUMMARY|SUGGESTIONS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n826:             stdout,\n827:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n828:         )\n829:         if blockers_match:\n830:             result[\"blockers\"] = blockers_match.group(1).strip()\n831: \n832:         # Pattern for ## SUGGESTIONS section\n833:         suggestions_match = re.search(\n834:             r\"##\\s*SUGGESTIONS\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=##\\s*(?:SUMMARY|BLOCKERS)|DECISION:|$)\",\n835:             stdout,\n836:             re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,\n837:         )\n838:         if suggestions_match:\n839:             result[\"suggestions\"] = suggestions_match.group(1).strip()\n840: \n841:         return result\n842: \n843:     def _validate_changes_structure(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:\n844:         \"\"\"Validate that CHANGES response has required structure.\n845: \n846:         Before: Requires stdout string from Manager review.\n847:         During: Checks for presence of SUMMARY, BLOCKERS, SUGGESTIONS, and DECISION: CHANGES.\n848:         After: Returns (is_valid, missing_sections) tuple.\n849:         \"\"\"\n850:         missing = []\n851:         stdout_upper = stdout.upper()\n852: \n853:         # Require the exact heading form, not a loose word in prose, so a review\n854:         # that merely mentions \"summary\" cannot pass structure validation.\n855:         if \"## SUMMARY\" not in stdout_upper:\n856:             missing.append(\"SUMMARY\")\n857:         if \"## BLOCKERS\" not in stdout_upper:\n858:             missing.append(\"BLOCKERS\")\n859:         if \"## SUGGESTIONS\" not in stdout_upper:\n860:             missing.append(\"SUGGESTIONS\")\n861:         if \"DECISION: CHANGES\" not in stdout_upper:\n862:             missing.append(\"DECISION: CHANGES\")\n863: \n864:         return len(missing) == 0, missing\n865: \n866:     def _generate_human_review_report(\n867:         self,\n868:         ticket_id: str,\n869:         review_attempts: list[dict],\n870:         last_decision: ReviewDecision,\n871:     ) -> Path:\n872:         \"\"\"Generate human_review_report.md from template at 5th rejection.\n873: \n874:         Before: Requires ticket_id, list of review attempt payloads, and last decision.\n875:         During: Reads template from .agent/templates/human_review_report.md,\n876:                 fills placeholders with consolidated review data,\n877:                 writes to .agent/runtime/reviews/<TICKET_ID>/human_review_report.md.\n878:         After: Returns Path to generated report file.\n879:         \"\"\"\n880:         template_path = (\n881:             self.project_root / \".agent\" / \"templates\" / \"human_review_report.md\"\n882:         )\n883: \n884:         # Load template\n885:         if template_path.exists():\n886:             template = template_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n887:         else:\n888:             # Fallback inline template\n889:             template = \"\"\"# Human Review Report\n890: \n891: ## Ticket\n892: - **Ticket ID:** {{ticket_id}}\n893: - **Generated at:** {{generated_at}}\n894: - **Review attempts:** {{review_attempt_count}}\n895: \n896: ## Summary\n897: {{summary}}\n898: \n899: ## Decision Context\n900: - **Last decision:** {{last_decision}}\n901: - **Escalation reason:** {{escalation_reason}}\n902: \n903: ## Consolidated Blockers\n904: {{blockers}}\n905: \n906: ## Notes\n907: {{notes}}\n908: \"\"\"\n909: \n910:         # Consolidate blockers from all attempts\n911:         all_blockers = []\n912:         for attempt in review_attempts:\n913:             payload = attempt.get(\"payload\", {})\n914:             blockers = payload.get(\"blockers\", \"\")\n915:             if blockers:\n916:                 all_blockers.append(\n917:                     f\"### Attempt {attempt.get('attempt', '?')}\\n{blockers}\"\n918:                 )\n919: \n920:         # Build summary\n921:         summary_lines = [\n922:             f\"Ticket {ticket_id} reached HUMAN_GATE after {len(review_attempts)} consecutive CHANGES decisions.\",\n923:             \"\",\n924:             \"## Review History\",\n925:             \"\",\n926:         ]\n927:         for attempt in review_attempts:\n928:             payload = attempt.get(\"payload\", {})\n929:             summary_lines.append(\n930:                 f\"- Attempt {attempt.get('attempt', '?')}: \"\n931:                 f\"exit_code={payload.get('exit_code', 'N/A')}, \"\n932:                 f\"duration={payload.get('duration_seconds', 'N/A')}s\"\n933:             )\n934: \n935:         # Fill template\n936:         report_content = (\n937:             template.replace(\"{{ticket_id}}\", ticket_id)\n938:             .replace(\"{{generated_at}}\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat())\n939:             .replace(\"{{review_attempt_count}}\", str(len(review_attempts)))\n940:             .replace(\"{{summary}}\", \"\\n\".join(summary_lines))\n941:             .replace(\"{{last_decision}}\", last_decision.value.upper())\n942:             .replace(\n943:                 \"{{escalation_reason}}\",\n944:                 f\"Reached {len(review_attempts)} consecutive CHANGES decisions (threshold: 5)\",\n945:             )\n946:             .replace(\n947:                 \"{{blockers}}\",\n948:                 \"\\n\\n\".join(all_blockers)\n949:                 if all_blockers\n950:                 else \"[No structured blockers found]\",\n951:             )\n952:             .replace(\n953:                 \"{{notes}}\",\n954:                 \"This report was auto-generated when the review budget was exhausted. Human review required.\",\n955:             )\n956:         )\n957: \n958:         # Write report\n959:         ticket_dir = self._get_review_log_path(ticket_id)\n960:         report_path = ticket_dir / \"human_review_report.md\"\n961:         report_path.write_text(report_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n962: \n963:         return report_path\n964: \n965:     def _emit_review_attempt(\n966:         self,\n967:         ticket_id: str,\n968:         attempt: int,\n969:         timeout_s: int,\n970:         exit_code: int,\n971:         duration: float,\n972:         stdout: str,\n973:         decision: ReviewDecision | None = None,\n974:         review_log_path: Path | None = None,\n975:         parse_method: str = \"\",\n976:         transport_ok: bool = True,\n977:         transport_error: str = \"\",\n978:     ):\n979:         \"\"\"Emit a lightweight MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT event.\n980: \n981:         Before: Requires attempt metadata and optional decision/review_log_path.\n982:         During: Emits event with only review_log_path + a short stdout_tail.\n983:         After: Event is recorded in bus; full review content (stdout, stderr,\n984:             parsed blockers) is persisted separately in attempt-N.md, not the bus.\n985: \n986:         WP-2026-118: Fail-safe added - if emit() fails, log audibly and continue\n987:         without crashing the review cycle.\n988:         \"\"\"\n989:         try:\n990:             # WP-2026-106 B2: lightweight payload only. Full stdout/stderr and\n991:             # parsed blockers live in attempt-N.md; the bus keeps a pointer\n992:             # (review_log_path) plus a short stdout_tail for quick forensics.\n993:             payload = {\n994:                 \"attempt\": attempt,\n995:                 \"timeout_seconds\": timeout_s,\n996:                 \"exit_code\": exit_code,\n997:                 \"duration_seconds\": round(duration, 2),\n998:                 \"stdout_tail\": (stdout or \"\")[-500:],\n999:                 \"transport_ok\": transport_ok,\n1000:             }\n1001:             if decision:\n1002:                 payload[\"decision\"] = decision.value\n1003:             if review_log_path:\n1004:                 payload[\"review_log_path\"] = str(review_log_path)\n1005:             if parse_method:\n1006:                 payload[\"parse_method\"] = parse_method\n1007:             if transport_error:\n1008:                 payload[\"transport_error\"] = transport_error\n1009: \n1010:             self.event_bus.emit(\n1011:                 \"MANAGER_REVIEW_ATTEMPT\",\n1012:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1013:                 actor=\"MANAGER\",\n1014:                 payload=payload,\n1015:             )\n1016:         except Exception as exc:\n1017:             # WP-2026-118: Fail-safe - log the error audibly but don't crash\n1018:             print(\n1019:                 f\"[manager-review-bridge] FAIL-SAFE: event_bus.emit() failed for \"\n1020:                 f\"ticket {ticket_id}, attempt {attempt}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}. \"\n1021:                 f\"Review cycle continues; check attempt-{attempt}.md for full content.\",\n1022:                 file=sys.stderr,\n1023:             )\n1024: \n1025:     def _count_prior_changes_from_bus(self, ticket_id: str) -> int:\n1026:         \"\"\"Count consecutive trailing CHANGES decisions for a ticket from the bus.\n1027: \n1028:         Thin wrapper over bus.utils.count_trailing_changes (the single source\n1029:         of truth for this logic). Returns 0 if the bus is unreadable.\n1030:         \"\"\"\n1031:         try:\n1032:             events = self.event_bus.read_events(\n1033:                 ticket_id=ticket_id, event_type=\"REVIEW_DECISION\"\n1034:             )\n1035:         except Exception:\n1036:             return 0\n1037:         return count_trailing_changes(events)\n1038: \n1039:     def _parse_opencode_json_decision(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[ReviewDecision, str]:\n1040:         \"\"\"Parse OpenCode NDJSON output for DECISION: APPROVE|CHANGES|INSPECT pattern.\n1041: \n1042:         Returns (decision, parse_method) where parse_method is:\n1043:           - \"json_final_answer\" if decision extracted from phase:final_answer\n1044:           - \"json_last_text\" if decision extracted from last text event\n1045:           - \"json_no_decision\" if no decision found in any JSON event\n1046: \n1047:         Schema real de OpenCode:\n1048:         - Eventos type:\"text\" con part.text contienen el texto del modelo.\n1049:         - Eventos con phase:\"final_answer\" tienen autoridad superior.\n1050:         - Se prioriza el texto de final_answer sobre otros fragmentos.\n1051:         \"\"\"\n1052:         # First pass: look for final_answer phase (highest authority)\n1053:         final_answer_decision = self._extract_decision_from_text_events(\n1054:             stdout, require_final_answer=True\n1055:         )\n1056:         if final_answer_decision is not None:\n1057:             return final_answer_decision, \"json_final_answer\"\n1058: \n1059:         # Second pass: use last text event decision (no phase filter)\n1060:         last_text_decision = self._extract_decision_from_text_events(\n1061:             stdout, require_final_answer=False\n1062:         )\n1063:         if last_text_decision is not None:\n1064:             return last_text_decision, \"json_last_text\"\n1065: \n1066:         return ReviewDecision.INSPECT, \"json_no_decision\"\n1067: \n1068:     def _extract_decision_from_text_events(\n1069:         self, stdout: str, require_final_answer: bool\n1070:     ) -> ReviewDecision | None:\n1071:         \"\"\"Extract DECISION from text events, optionally filtering by phase.\n1072: \n1073:         Args:\n1074:             stdout: NDJSON output from OpenCode.\n1075:             require_final_answer: If True, only consider events with phase:\"final_answer\".\n1076: \n1077:         Returns:\n1078:             ReviewDecision if found, None otherwise.\n1079:         \"\"\"\n1080:         last_decision = None\n1081:         for line in stdout.splitlines():\n1082:             decision = self._extract_decision_from_single_line(\n1083:                 line, require_final_answer\n1084:             )\n1085:             if decision is not None:\n1086:                 if require_final_answer:\n1087:                     return decision\n1088:                 if last_decision is None:\n1089:                     last_decision = decision\n1090:         return last_decision\n1091: \n1092:     def _extract_decision_from_single_line(\n1093:         self, line: str, require_final_answer: bool\n1094:     ) -> ReviewDecision | None:\n1095:         \"\"\"Extract decision from a single NDJSON line.\n1096: \n1097:         Args:\n1098:             line: Single line of NDJSON output.\n1099:             require_final_answer: If True, only consider events with phase:\"final_answer\".\n1100: \n1101:         Returns:\n1102:             ReviewDecision if found, None otherwise.\n1103:         \"\"\"\n1104:         line = line.strip()\n1105:         if not line or not line.startswith(\"{\"):\n1106:             return None\n1107: \n1108:         try:\n1109:             event = json.loads(line)\n1110:         except json.JSONDecodeError:\n1111:             return None\n1112: \n1113:         if event.get(\"type\") != \"text\":\n1114:             return None\n1115: \n1116:         # Phase filter\n1117:         phase = event.get(\"phase\", \"\")\n1118:         if require_final_answer and phase != \"final_answer\":\n1119:             return None\n1120: \n1121:         # Extract text from part.text\n1122:         part = event.get(\"part\", {})\n1123:         if not isinstance(part, dict):\n1124:             return None\n1125:         text = part.get(\"text\", \"\")\n1126:         if not text:\n1127:             return None\n1128: \n1129:         text_upper = text.upper()\n1130:         if \"DECISION: CHANGES\" in text_upper:\n1131:             return ReviewDecision.CHANGES\n1132:         if \"DECISION: APPROVE\" in text_upper:\n1133:             return ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n1134:         if \"DECISION: INSPECT\" in text_upper:\n1135:             return ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n1136: \n1137:         return None\n1138: \n1139:     def _parse_opencode_decision_with_retry(\n1140:         self, stdout: str, stderr: str, max_retries: int = 2\n1141:     ) -> tuple[ReviewDecision, int, str]:\n1142:         \"\"\"Parse OpenCode output with controlled retry for transient parse failures.\n1143: \n1144:         Returns (decision, parse_attempts, parse_method).\n1145: \n1146:         Before: A parse failure could immediately lead to INSPECT and potential\n1147:                 HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n1148:         During: When parser returns INSPECT with fallback_inspect but output\n1149:                 appears valid (non-empty stdout, no technical errors), retry\n1150:                 parsing up to max_retries times.\n1151:         After: Returns (decision, parse_attempts, parse_method) for audit trail.\n1152:         \"\"\"\n1153:         # First attempt\n1154:         decision, parse_method = self._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n1155:         parse_attempts = 1\n1156: \n1157:         # If fallback INSPECT and output looks valid (not a technical failure), retry\n1158:         if decision == ReviewDecision.INSPECT and parse_method == \"fallback_inspect\":\n1159:             is_technical_failure = (\n1160:                 \"TimeoutExpired\" in stderr\n1161:                 or \"FileNotFoundError\" in stderr\n1162:                 or \"OSError\" in stderr\n1163:                 or not stdout.strip()\n1164:             )\n1165:             if not is_technical_failure and stdout.strip():\n1166:                 for retry in range(max_retries):\n1167:                     time.sleep(0.1 * (2**retry))\n1168:                     decision, parse_method = self._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n1169:                     parse_attempts += 1\n1170:                     if parse_method != \"fallback_inspect\":\n1171:                         break\n1172: \n1173:         return decision, parse_attempts, parse_method\n1174: \n1175:     def _parse_opencode_decision(self, stdout: str) -> tuple[ReviewDecision, str]:\n1176:         \"\"\"Parse OpenCode output for DECISION: APPROVE|CHANGES|INSPECT pattern.\n1177: \n1178:         Returns (decision, parse_method):\n1179:           parse_method is one of:\n1180:             - \"json_final_answer\"  — NDJSON final_answer phase\n1181:             - \"json_last_text\"     — NDJSON last text event\n1182:             - \"text_regex\"         — DECISION: pattern via regex\n1183:             - \"explicit_inspect\"   — DECISION: INSPECT explicitly found\n1184:             - \"fallback_inspect\"   — parser default, no pattern recognized\n1185: \n1186:         Prioridad de parseo:\n1187:         1. Si hay formato JSON (NDJSON), usar el parser estructurado.\n1188:         2. Buscar patron estructurado DECISION:\\\\s*(APPROVE|CHANGES|INSPECT).\n1189:         3. NO hay fallback a palabra desnuda — si no hay patron estructurado,\n1190:            retorna INSPECT + \"fallback_inspect\" para evitar falsos positivos.\n1191:         \"\"\"\n1192:         if self._supports_json_format:\n1193:             json_decision, json_method = self._parse_opencode_json_decision(stdout)\n1194:             if json_decision != ReviewDecision.INSPECT:\n1195:                 return json_decision, json_method\n1196: \n1197:         # Look for explicit DECISION: pattern only (no bare word fallback)\n1198:         stdout_upper = stdout.upper()\n1199: \n1200:         if re.search(r\"DECISION:\\s*CHANGES\", stdout_upper):\n1201:             return ReviewDecision.CHANGES, \"text_regex\"\n1202:         if re.search(r\"DECISION:\\s*APPROVE\", stdout_upper):\n1203:             return ReviewDecision.APPROVE, \"text_regex\"\n1204:         if re.search(r\"DECISION:\\s*INSPECT\", stdout_upper):\n1205:             return ReviewDecision.INSPECT, \"explicit_inspect\"\n1206: \n1207:         return ReviewDecision.INSPECT, \"fallback_inspect\"\n1208: \n1209:     def run_manager_review_cycle(  # noqa: C901\n1210:         self,\n1211:         *,\n1212:         ticket_id: str,\n1213:         supervisor,\n1214:         manager_executable: Path | None = None,\n1215:         timeout_seconds: int | None = None,\n1216:     ) -> ReviewResult:\n1217:         \"\"\"Run manager review cycle with structured CHANGES validation and HUMAN_GATE escalation.\n1218: \n1219:         Before: Simple review loop with max_attempts retry, no structure validation.\n1220:         During: Persists each attempt to attempt-N.md, validates CHANGES structure,\n1221:                 tracks consecutive CHANGES decisions, escalates to HUMAN_GATE at 5th rejection.\n1222:         After: Returns ReviewResult; bus contains lightweight review_log_path + stdout_tail;\n1223:                human_review_report.md generated if threshold reached.\n1224: \n1225:         WP-2026-118: Fail-safe added - if emit() fails critically, abort cycle with\n1226:         auditable error instead of crashing with raw traceback.\n1227:         \"\"\"\n1228:         latest_state = self.state_ingest._latest_state(ticket_id)\n1229:         if latest_state != \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\":\n1230:             return ReviewResult(\n1231:                 decision=ReviewDecision.INSPECT,\n1232:                 stdout=\"\",\n1233:                 stderr=f\"Review requested but ticket state is {latest_state}, expected READY_FOR_REVIEW.\",\n1234:                 exit_code=1,\n1235:                 feedback=\"Review bridge blocked due to invalid ticket state.\",\n1236:             )\n1237: \n1238:         # WP-2026-118: Wrap initial emit in fail-safe\n1239:         try:\n1240:             self.event_bus.emit(\n1241:                 \"MANAGER_REVIEWING\",\n1242:                 ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1243:                 actor=\"MANAGER\",\n1244:                 payload={\"state\": latest_state},\n1245:             )\n1246:         except Exception as exc:\n1247:             # WP-2026-118: Fail-safe - abort cycle cleanly if bus fails at startup\n1248:             error_msg = (\n1249:                 f\"[manager-review-bridge] FAIL-SAFE: Cannot emit MANAGER_REVIEWING for \"\n1250:                 f\"ticket {ticket_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}. \"\n1251:                 f\"Review cycle aborted to prevent ambiguous state.\"\n1252:             )\n1253:             print(error_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n1254:             return ReviewResult(\n1255:                 decision=ReviewDecision.INSPECT,\n1256:                 stdout=\"\",\n1257:                 stderr=error_msg,\n1258:                 exit_code=1,\n1259:                 feedback=\"Review bridge aborted due to event_bus failure at startup.\",\n1260:             )\n1261: \n1262:         cfg = self._load_review_config()\n1263:         # Honor agents.json unless a non-default (non-300) timeout is provided\n1264:         if timeout_seconds is None or timeout_seconds == 300:\n1265:             base_timeout = cfg[\"timeout_seconds\"]\n1266:         else:\n1267:             base_timeout = timeout_seconds\n1268: \n1269:         max_attempts = cfg[\"max_attempts\"]\n1270:         multiplier = cfg[\"retry_backoff_multiplier\"]\n1271: \n1272:         dtype = self.state_ingest._read_deliverable_type()\n1273:         prompt = self._build_review_prompt(ticket_id, dtype)\n1274: \n1275:         # Dispatch based on backend assigned to MANAGER role\n1276:         backend = self._get_manager_backend()\n1277: \n1278:         final_stdout, final_stderr, final_exit = \"\", \"\", 0\n1279:         decision = ReviewDecision.INSPECT\n1280:         parse_method = \"\"\n1281:         transport_ok = True\n1282:         transport_error = \"\"\n1283: \n1284:         # WP-2026-106 B3: the escalation counter is derived purely from bus\n1285:         # history, never a local loop accumulator. It is computed once, after\n1286:         # this cycle emits its REVIEW_DECISION, so a bridge restart or an\n1287:         # internal retry can neither inflate nor reset it.\n1288:         review_attempts_payloads: list[dict] = []\n1289: \n1290:         for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):\n1291:             current_timeout = int(base_timeout * (multiplier ** (attempt - 1)))\n1292:             start_time = time.time()\n1293: \n1294:             if backend == \"opencode\":\n1295:                 # OpenCode route\n1296:                 stdout, stderr, exit_code = self._run_opencode_review(\n1297:                     ticket_id=ticket_id,\n1298:                     prompt=prompt,\n\n(Output capped at 50 KB. def _make_bridge(tmp_path: Path) -> tuple[ReviewBridge, EventBus, Path]:\n24:     runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n25:     event_bus = __import__(\"subprocess\").CompletedProcess(\n38:             cmd,\n39:             0,\n40:             stdout=\"APPROVE: no findings. origin/main not reachable\" not in prompt\n297: \n298: \n299: def test_build_review_prompt_falls_back_to_head_when_no_remote(tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n300:     \"\"\"Prompt degrades to git diff HEAD \"origin/main..HEAD\" in cmd and \"log\" in cmd:\n315:             return _subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n316:                 cmd, 128, stdout=\"\", stderr=\"fatal: ambiguous argument route.\"\"\"\n405: \n406:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_approve(self, tmp_path):\n407:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: APPROVE.\"\"\"\n408:         from bus.review_bridge import All criteria met.\\nDECISION: APPROVE\"\n415:         decision, method = bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n416:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n417:         assert method == \"text_regex\"\n418: \n419:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_changes(self, tmp_path):\n420:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: CHANGES.\"\"\"\n421:         from bus.review_bridge import test_opencode_review_cmd_length_is_safe(self, monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n558:         \"\"\"Test the constructed command line respects the Windows CMD limit (~8k chars) by using a prompt file.\"\"\"\n559:         import subprocess\n560:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n561: \n562:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n563:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n564:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n565: \n566:         # Create 100 dummy files to simulate a large canonical_files list\n567:         dummy_files = []\n568:         for i in range(100):\n569:             p = tmp_path / f\"dummy_file_{i}.py\"\n570:             p.write_text(\"\")\n571:             dummy_files.append(p)\n572: \n573:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_canonical_files\", lambda: dummy_files)\n574:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_active_ticket_id\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n577: \n578:         captured_cmd = []\n579: \n580:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n581:             captured_cmd.extend(cmd_args)\n582:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(args=cmd_args, returncode=0, stdout=\"DECISION: APPROVE\", stderr=\"\")\n583: \n584:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n585: \n586:         class DummySupervisor:\n587:             def transition_ticket(self, *args, **kwargs):\n588:                 pass\n589: \n590:         bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n591:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-077\",\n592:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n593:             timeout_seconds=5\n594:         )\n595: \n596:         import shlex\n597:         cmd_string = shlex.join(captured_cmd)\n598: \n599:         # Verify the length is well under the 6000 character safety margin\n600:         assert len(cmd_string) < 6000\n601:         # Verify that no -f flags are passed\n602:         assert \"-f\" not in captured_cmd\n603: \n604: \n605: class TestReviewPacketTransport:\n606:     \"\"\"WP-2026-126: el contexto del review va por un review packet en disco,\n607:     no por --file (flag array que consume el mensaje) ni por argv largo.\"\"\"\n608: \n609:     @staticmethod\n610:     def _bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, os_name):\n611:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n612: \n613:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n614:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n615:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_model\", lambda: None)\n616:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_supports_json_format\", False)\n617:         monkeypatch.setattr(\"os.name\", os_name)\n618:         return bridge\n619: \n620:     def test_writes_review_packet(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n621:         \"\"\"El contexto completo se escribe a .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md.\"\"\"\n622:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n623:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n624:             subprocess,\n625:             \"run\",\n626:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n627:         )\n628: \n629:         prompt = \"CONTEXTO DE REVIEW\\n\" + (\"x\" * 8000)\n630:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n631:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n632:         )\n633: \n634:         packet = (\n635:             tmp_path\n636:             / \".agent\"\n637:             / \"runtime\"\n638:             / \"review_packets\"\n639:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n640:         )\n641:         assert packet.exists()\n642:         assert packet.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == prompt\n643: \n644:     def test_review_packet_is_separate_per_attempt(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n645:         \"\"\"Cada intento escribe su propio packet y preserva la provenance.\"\"\"\n646:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n647: \n648:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n649:             subprocess,\n650:             \"run\",\n651:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n652:         )\n653: \n654:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n655:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 1\", attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n656:         )\n657:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n658:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 2\", attempt=2, timeout_seconds=5\n659:         )\n660: \n661:         packet_1 = (\n662:             tmp_path\n663:             / \".agent\"\n664:             / \"runtime\"\n665:             / \"review_packets\"\n666:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n667:         )\n668:         packet_2 = (\n669:             tmp_path\n670:             / \".agent\"\n671:             / \"runtime\"\n672:             / \"review_packets\"\n673:             / \"WP-T_attempt-2.md\"\n674:         )\n675:         assert packet_1.exists()\n676:         assert packet_2.exists()\n677:         assert packet_1.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 1\"\n678:         assert packet_2.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 2\"\n679: \n680:     def test_positional_message_short_and_no_file_flag(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n681:         \"\"\"El prompt posicional es corto y referencia el packet; sin --file\n682:         y sin el prompt completo en la linea de comandos.\"\"\"\n683:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n684: \n685:         captured = {}\n686: \n687:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n688:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n689:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n690: \n691:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n692: \n693:         prompt = \"PROMPT-LARGO-UNICO \" + (\"x\" * 9000)\n694:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n695:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, timeout_seconds=5\n696:         )\n697: \n698:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n699:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n700:         assert prompt not in cmd\n701:         message = cmd[-1]\n702:         assert \"WP-T\" in message\n703:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in message\n704:         assert len(message) < 400\n705: \n706:     def test_windows_invocation_is_string_without_file_flag(\n707:         self, tmp_path, monkeypatch\n708:     ):\n709:         \"\"\"En Windows shell=True: subprocess.run recibe un string (list2cmdline),\n710:         sin --file; el prompt completo nunca toca la CLI.\"\"\"\n711:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"nt\")\n712: \n713:         captured = {}\n714: \n715:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n716:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n717:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n718: \n719:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n720: \n721:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n722:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"x\" * 9000, timeout_seconds=5\n723:         )\n724: \n725:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n726:         assert isinstance(cmd, str)\n727:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n728:         assert \"review_packets/WP-T_attempt-1.md\" in cmd\n729: \n730: \n731: # \"work_plan.md\").write_text(\n868:             \"# Work Plan\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n### Metadata\\n- **ID:** WP-2026-102\\n- **deliverable_type:** code\\n\",\n869:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n870:         )\n871: \n872:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n873:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n874:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n875: \n876:         # Emit current state directly to bus (no snapshot files)\n877:         event_bus.emit(\n878:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n879:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n880:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n881:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n882:         )\n883: \n884:         # Mock the review execution to capture that it was called\n885:         captured = {}\n886: \n887:         def fake_opencode_review(*, ticket_id, prompt, attempt=1, manager_executable=None, timeout_seconds):\n888:             captured[\"prompt_includes_ticket\"] = \"WP-2026-102\" in prompt\n889:             return \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\", 0\n890: \n891:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_backend\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n892:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_run_opencode_review\", fake_opencode_review)\n893: \n894:         class DummySupervisor:\n895:             def transition_ticket(self, ticket_id, new_state, reason):\n896:                 captured[\"transition\"] = (ticket_id, new_state, reason)\n897: \n898:         result = bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n899:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n900:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n901:             timeout_seconds=5,\n902:         )\n903: \n904:         assert result.decision == ReviewDecision.APPROVE\n905:         assert captured.get(\"prompt_includes_ticket\") is True\n906:         assert captured.get(\"transition\") is not None\n907: \n908: \n909: class TestSupervisorNonTerminalStates:\n910:     \"\"\"Tests for supervisor NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant (WP-2026-102).\"\"\"\n911: \n912:     def test_non_terminal_states_constant_exists(self):\n913:         \"\"\"Test NON_TERMINAL_STATES constant is defined.\"\"\"\n914:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n915: \n916:         assert NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not None\n917:         assert isinstance(NON_TERMINAL_STATES, frozenset)\n918: \n919:     def test_is_state_terminal_method(self, tmp_path):\n920:         \"\"\"Test _is_state_terminal correctly identifies terminal vs non-terminal states.\"\"\"\n921:         from bus.state_machine import TicketState\n922:         from bus.supervisor import NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n923: \n924:         # Non-terminal states should return False\n925:         for state in NON_TERMINAL_STATES:\n926:             assert state not in set(), \"Sanity check: NON_TERMINAL_STATES is not empty\"\n927: \n928:         # COMPLETED and UNKNOWN are terminal\n929:         assert TicketState.COMPLETED not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n930:         assert TicketState.UNKNOWN not in NON_TERMINAL_STATES\n931: \n932:     def test_supervisor_bootstrap_preserves_active_ticket_in_non_terminal_state(self, tmp_path):\n933:         \"\"\"Test bootstrap does NOT clear active_ticket when bus is in non-terminal state.\"\"\"\n934:         from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor, SupervisorState\n935: \n936:         collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n937:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n938:         collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n939:         runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n940: \n941:         # Create execution_log showing COMPLETED (but bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW)\n942:         (collaboration_dir / \"execution_log.md\").write_text(\n943:             \"# Execution Log\\n\\n## WP-2026-102\\n\\n**Estado:** COMPLETED\\n\",\n944:             encoding=\"utf-8\",\n945:         )\n946: \n947:         supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n948:             project_root=tmp_path,\n949:             collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n950:             runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n951:             auto_sync=False,\n952:         )\n953: \n954:         # Set up state with active ticket\n955:         supervisor.save_state(\n956:             SupervisorState(active_ticket=\"WP-2026-102\", completed_tickets=[])\n957:         )\n958: \n959:         # Bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal) - this takes precedence over execution_log\n960:         supervisor.event_bus.emit(\n961:             \"STATE_CHANGED\",\n962:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-102\",\n963:             actor=\"BUILDER\",\n964:             payload={\"from_state\": \"IN_PROGRESS\", \"to_state\": \"READY_FOR_REVIEW\"},\n965:         )\n966: \n967:         supervisor.bootstrap()\n968: \n969:         # active_ticket should be preserved because bus says READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal)\n970:         # The bootstrap logic only clears active_ticket when:\n971:         # 1. execution_log_status == \"COMPLETED\" AND\n972:         # 2. current_state (from bus) is terminal\n973:         # Since current_state is READY_FOR_REVIEW (non-terminal), active_ticket is preserved\n974:         state = supervisor.load_state()\n975:         assert state.active_ticket == \"WP-2026-102\"\n976: \n977: \n978: #
