6 events in bus\n923:      assert state.loop_current_round == 2\n924: +\n925: +\n926: +# =============================================================================\n927: +# Tests WP-2026-137: Supervisor startup lock and reconciliation dedupe\n928: +# =============================================================================\n929: +\n930: +\n931: +def test_supervisor_lock_acquire_atomic(tmp_path):\n932: +    \"\"\"Test that supervisor lock is acquired atomically.\"\"\"\n933: +    import json\n934: +    import os\n935: +\n936: +    from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor\n937: +\n938: +    collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n939: +    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n940: +    collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n941: +    runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n942: +\n943: +    supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n944: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n945: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n946: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n947: +        auto_sync=False,\n948: +    )\n949: +\n950: +    # Acquire lock\n951: +    result = supervisor._acquire_supervisor_lock()\n952: +    assert result is True\n953: +    assert supervisor._lock_fd is not None\n954: +    assert supervisor.supervisor_lock_path.exists()\n955: +\n956: +    # Verify lock content\n957: +    lock_data = json.loads(supervisor.supervisor_lock_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n958: +    assert lock_data[\"pid\"] == os.getpid()\n959: +    assert \"started_at\" in lock_data\n960: +\n961: +    # Release lock\n962: +    supervisor._release_supervisor_lock()\n963: +    assert not supervisor.supervisor_lock_path.exists()\n964: +\n965: +\n966: +def test_supervisor_lock_contention(tmp_path):\n967: +    \"\"\"Test that second supervisor instance cannot acquire lock when first holds it.\"\"\"\n968: +    from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor\n969: +\n970: +    collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n971: +    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n972: +    collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n973: +    runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n974: +\n975: +    supervisor1 = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n976: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n977: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n978: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n979: +        auto_sync=False,\n980: +    )\n981: +\n982: +    supervisor2 = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n983: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n984: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n985: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n986: +        auto_sync=False,\n987: +    )\n988: +\n989: +    # First supervisor acquires lock\n990: +    result1 = supervisor1._acquire_supervisor_lock()\n991: +    assert result1 is True\n992: +\n993: +    # Second supervisor should fail to acquire lock\n994: +    result2 = supervisor2._acquire_supervisor_lock()\n995: +    assert result2 is False\n996: +\n997: +    # Release lock from first supervisor\n998: +    supervisor1._release_supervisor_lock()\n999: +\n1000: +    # Now second supervisor should be able to acquire\n1001: +    result2_retry = supervisor2._acquire_supervisor_lock()\n1002: +    assert result2_retry is True\n1003: +\n1004: +    supervisor2._release_supervisor_lock()\n1005: +\n1006: +\n1007: +def test_supervisor_lock_stale_by_mtime(tmp_path):\n1008: +    \"\"\"Test that stale lock (old mtime) is broken and re-acquired.\"\"\"\n1009: +    import json\n1010: +    import os\n1011: +    import time\n1012: +\n1013: +    from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor\n1014: +\n1015: +    collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n1016: +    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n1017: +    collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1018: +    runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1019: +\n1020: +    supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n1021: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n1022: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n1023: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n1024: +        auto_sync=False,\n1025: +    )\n1026: +\n1027: +    # Create a fake old lock file\n1028: +    lock_path = supervisor.supervisor_lock_path\n1029: +    lock_data = {\n1030: +        \"pid\": 999999,  # Dead PID\n1031: +        \"ticket_id\": \"WP-OLD\",\n1032: +        \"started_at\": \"2020-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n1033: +    }\n1034: +    lock_path.write_text(json.dumps(lock_data), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n1035: +\n1036: +    # Make lock file old (>15 min)\n1037: +    old_time = time.time() - 1000\n1038: +    os.utime(lock_path, (old_time, old_time))\n1039: +\n1040: +    # Should break stale lock and acquire successfully\n1041: +    result = supervisor._acquire_supervisor_lock()\n1042: +    assert result is True\n1043: +\n1044: +    # Verify new lock has current PID\n1045: +    new_lock_data = json.loads(lock_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n1046: +    assert new_lock_data[\"pid\"] == os.getpid()\n1047: +\n1048: +    supervisor._release_supervisor_lock()\n1049: +\n1050: +\n1051: +def test_bootstrap_rejects_duplicate_instance(tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n1052: +    \"\"\"Test that bootstrap returns False when another instance holds the lock.\"\"\"\n1053: +    from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor\n1054: +\n1055: +    collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n1056: +    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n1057: +    collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1058: +    runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1059: +\n1060: +    supervisor1 = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n1061: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n1062: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n1063: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n1064: +        auto_sync=False,\n1065: +    )\n1066: +\n1067: +    supervisor2 = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n1068: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n1069: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n1070: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n1071: +        auto_sync=False,\n1072: +    )\n1073: +\n1074: +    # First supervisor acquires lock via bootstrap\n1075: +    result1 = supervisor1.bootstrap()\n1076: +    assert result1 is True\n1077: +\n1078: +    # Second supervisor should be rejected\n1079: +    result2 = supervisor2.bootstrap()\n1080: +    assert result2 is False\n1081: +\n1082: +    # Clean up\n1083: +    supervisor1._release_supervisor_lock()\n1084: +\n1085: +\n1086: +def test_bootstrap_reconciled_deduplication(tmp_path):\n1087: +    \"\"\"Test that SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED is not emitted twice for same pair.\"\"\"\n1088: +    from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor, SupervisorState\n1089: +\n1090: +    collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n1091: +    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n1092: +    collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1093: +    runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1094: +\n1095: +    # Create TURN.md with ticket\n1096: +    turn_path = collaboration_dir / \"TURN.md\"\n1097: +    turn_path.write_text(\n1098: +        \"\\n\".join(\n1099: +            [\n1100: +                \"# TURNO ACTUAL\",\n1101: +                \"\",\n1102: +                \"| Campo | Valor |\",\n1103: +                \"|-------|-------|\",\n1104: +                \"| **ROL** | **BUILDER** |\",\n1105: +                \"| **Plan ID** | WP-2026-137 |\",\n1106: +                \"| **Accion** | IMPLEMENT |\",\n1107: +            ]\n1108: +        ),\n1109: +        encoding=\"utf-8\",\n1110: +    )\n1111: +\n1112: +    supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n1113: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n1114: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n1115: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n1116: +        auto_sync=False,\n1117: +    )\n1118: +\n1119: +    # Start with stale state pointing to old ticket\n1120: +    supervisor.save_state(\n1121: +        SupervisorState(active_ticket=\"WP-2026-136\")\n1122: +    )\n1123: +\n1124: +    # First bootstrap - should emit SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED\n1125: +    result1 = supervisor.bootstrap()\n1126: +    assert result1 is True\n1127: +\n1128: +    events1 = supervisor.event_bus.read_events(\n1129: +        ticket_id=\"WP-2026-137\", event_type=\"SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED\"\n1130: +    )\n1131: +    assert len(events1) == 1\n1132: +    assert events1[0].payload[\"previous_ticket\"] == \"WP-2026-136\"\n1133: +    assert events1[0].payload[\"recovered_ticket\"] == \"WP-2026-137\"\n1134: +\n1135: +    # Release lock to simulate restart\n1136: +    supervisor._release_supervisor_lock()\n1137: +\n1138: +    # Second bootstrap with same state - should NOT emit again\n1139: +    result2 = supervisor.bootstrap()\n1140: +    assert result2 is True\n1141: +\n1142: +    events2 = supervisor.event_bus.read_events(\n1143: +        ticket_id=\"WP-2026-137\", event_type=\"SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED\"\n1144: +    )\n1145: +    # Still only 1 event - deduplication worked\n1146: +    assert len(events2) == 1\n1147: +\n1148: +    supervisor._release_supervisor_lock()\n1149: +\n1150: +\n1151: +def test_bootstrap_reconciled_different_pair_emits(tmp_path):\n1152: +    \"\"\"Test that SUPERVISOR_RECONCILED is emitted for different ticket pairs.\"\"\"\n1153: +    from bus.supervisor import SequentialTicketSupervisor, SupervisorState\n1154: +\n1155: +    collaboration_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"collaboration\"\n1156: +    runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\"\n1157: +    collaboration_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1158: +    runtime_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n1159: +\n1160: +    # Create TURN.md with ticket WP-2026-137\n1161: +    turn_path = collaboration_dir / \"TURN.md\"\n1162: +    turn_path.write_text(\n1163: +        \"\\n\".join(\n1164: +            [\n1165: +                \"# TURNO ACTUAL\",\n1166: +                \"\",\n1167: +                \"| Campo | Valor |\",\n1168: +                \"|-------|-------|\",\n1169: +                \"| **ROL** | **BUILDER** |\",\n1170: +                \"| **Plan ID** | WP-2026-137 |\",\n1171: +                \"| **Accion** | IMPLEMENT |\",\n1172: +            ]\n1173: +        ),\n1174: +        encoding=\"utf-8\",\n1175: +    )\n1176: +\n1177: +    supervisor = SequentialTicketSupervisor(\n1178: +        project_root=tmp_path,\n1179: +        collaboration_dir=collaboration_dir,\n1180: +        runtime_dir=runtime_dir,\n1181: +        auto_sync=False,\n1182: +    )\n1183: +\n1184: +    # `TURN.md`, `STATE.md` and `execution_log.md` stay synchronized together.\n39: - Builder and Manager are role contracts and may run on OpenCode or other approved backends.\n40: - `inspect` remains a human-gate projection of the Manager review path.\n41: \n42: ## Current readiness\n43: \n44: - Desacople de `project_root` en el motor\n52: - Auditar todas las rutas derivadas de `Path(__file__)` en el motor.\n53: - Parametrizar `project_root` de forma explicita en `agent_controller.py`, `bus/event_bus.py`, `bus/supervisor.py`, `bus/review_bridge.py`, `scripts/manager_review_bridge.py` y `scripts/local_audit.py` como minimo.\n54: - Mantener el comportamiento por defecto sin cambios cuando no se pase un root externo.\n55: - Resultado esperado: el motor puede operar sobre un destino externo sin copiarse dentro.\n56: \n57: ### WP-B - Workspace minimo en el destino\n58: - Hacer que el instalador cree solo el `.agent/` necesario en el proyecto destino.\n59: - No copiar el motor; solo estado, memoria y config del destino.\n60: - Definir y documentar el archivo de enlace motor-destino antes de este paso.\n61: \n62: ### WP-C - Launcher multi-root\n63: - Abrir motor y destino como workspace separados en VS Code.\n64: - Mantener el motor en `orquestador_de_agentes/` y el proyecto destino limpio.\n65: - La operacion debe seguir funcionando con el mismo contrato canonico de bus y colaboracion.\n66: \n67: ### WP-2026-124 - Mock run_once to raise exception on first call\n1294: +    call_count = 0\n1295: +\n1296: +    def flaky_run_once():\n1297: +        nonlocal call_count\n1298: +        call_count += 1\n1299: +        if call_count == 1:\n1300: +            raise RuntimeError(\"Simulated error\")\n1301: +        return False\n1302: +\n1303: +    monkeypatch.setattr(supervisor, \"run_once\", flaky_run_once)\n1304: +\n1305: +    # Mock time.sleep to exit quickly\n1306: +    sleep_count = 0\n1307: +\n1308: +    def quick_sleep(_seconds):\n1309: +        nonlocal sleep_count\n1310: +        sleep_count += 1\n1311: +        if sleep_count > 2:\n1312: +            raise KeyboardInterrupt(\"Stop loop\")\n1313: +\n1314: +    monkeypatch.setattr(\"time.sleep\", quick_sleep)\n1315: +\n1316: +    # Run loop should release lock even with exception\n1317: +    try:\n1318: +        supervisor.run_loop(poll_interval=0.1)\n1319: +    except (RuntimeError, KeyboardInterrupt):\n1320: +        pass\n1321: +\n1322: +    # Esto es una inconsistencia cosmética de estado que no afecta la implementación.\n\n---\n\nReview complete. All acceptance criteria met. Without it: BLOCKER.\n10: - Boolean truthiness regression in changed return contracts: if the diff changes a method's return type from implicit None to explicit bool, verify that every caller uses `is False` / `is True` rather than generic truthiness (`if not x`, `if x`, `while x`). Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. Any caller still using generic truthiness after the return-type change: BLOCKER.\n11: \n12: --- Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria ---\n13: Lecciones acumuladas de auditoria (de revisiones anteriores):\n14: - [2026-05-25] BLOCKER pattern: Exclusive resource acquisition without reentrancy guard. Add a safe loader for review learnings from `observations.jsonl`.\n141: 2. Filter only the observations tagged for manager review rubric learning.\n142: 3. Inject the dynamic section in `_build_review_prompt()` after the static rubric and before the canonical context blocks.\n143: 4. Sort by recency and cap the injected section at `MAX_RUBRIC_OBSERVATIONS = 5`.\n144: 5. Truncate each observation signal to `MAX_OBSERVATION_SIGNAL_CHARS = 200` characters when needed.\n145: 6. Keep the static rubric as the baseline and append the dynamic section as an augmentation for `code` and `mixed`.\n146: 7. Ensure missing, malformed, or empty memory degrades safely to \"no dynamic section\" rather than failing the review prompt.\n147: 8. Add regression tests for file absence, malformed JSON, topic filtering, cap enforcement, truncation, mixed coverage, and rubric preservation.\n148: \n149: ## Scope Notes\n150: \n151: - This ticket is read-only with respect to memory promotion: it consumes `observations.jsonl` but does not write to it.\n152: - It does not change the session-close observation pipeline.\n153: - The review decision contract stays `APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT`.\n154: - The dynamic section must stay compact and non-dominating so the baseline rubric remains primary.\n155: - The dynamic section must not alter the static rubric text or placement.\n156: \n157: ## Acceptance Shape\n158: \n159: - The Manager review prompt includes a dynamic observations section when matching learnings exist.\n160: - Only `topic=\"manager-review-rubric\"` entries are injected.\n161: - The section is ordered by recency and capped.\n162: - Each observation signal is truncated to 200 characters if needed.\n163: - The dynamic section applies to `code` and `mixed` prompts.\n164: - The prompt builder survives missing or invalid `observations.jsonl` without failing.\n165: - The static rubric and review decision contract are unchanged.\n166: - Tests cover the new behavior, rubric preservation, mixed coverage, and still pass.\n167: \n168: \n169: --- AUDIT_WP-2026-138.md ---\n170: # AUDIT_WP-2026-138 - Manager review memory injection from observations\n171: \n172: ## Audit Focus\n173: \n174: Verify that the review bridge can read curated learning from `observations.jsonl`, filter the right topic, and inject a bounded dynamic section into the Manager review prompt without breaking the static rubric or the decision contract.\n175: \n176: ## What Must Be True\n177: \n178: - `observations.jsonl` is treated as optional memory input, not a required runtime dependency.\n179: - Only observations with `topic=\"manager-review-rubric\"` are injected.\n180: - The injected section is ordered by recency and capped.\n181: - The cap is explicit and stable (`MAX_RUBRIC_OBSERVATIONS = 5`).\n182: - Each observation signal is truncated to 200 characters if needed.\n183: - The dynamic section is injected from `_build_review_prompt()` after the static rubric and before the canonical context blocks.\n184: - The dynamic section applies to `code` and `mixed` tickets.\n185: - The static rubric remains the baseline and is still present even when memory is empty.\n186: - The review prompt still ends up with the same `APPROVE / CHANGES / INSPECT` contract.\n187: - Missing, malformed, or empty memory does not make the review bridge fail.\n188: - The ticket does not mutate memory or alter the session-close pipeline.\n189: - The static rubric text is preserved, not reconstructed from memory.\n190: \n191: ## Failure Modes\n192: \n193: 1. The review prompt fails when `observations.jsonl` is missing or malformed.\n194: 2. Non-rubric observations leak into the Manager prompt.\n195: 3. The dynamic section grows without limit and crowds out the static rubric or diff.\n196: 4. Individual signals are inserted verbatim and make the prompt too long or noisy.\n197: 5. The code starts writing to `observations.jsonl` from the review bridge path.\n198: 6. The review decision contract changes as a side effect of the memory injection.\n199: 7. Tests only cover the happy path and miss empty-memory, malformed-memory, mixed, or rubric-preservation cases.\n200: \n201: ## Review Checklist\n202: \n203: - Confirm the loader handles absent and malformed memory safely.\n204: - Confirm the topic filter is exact and stable.\n205: - Confirm the section cap is enforced.\n206: - Confirm the truncation limit is enforced per observation.\n207: - Confirm the dynamic section is injected in `_build_review_prompt()` and not hidden inside rubric construction.\n208: - Confirm the dynamic section applies to both `code` and `mixed`.\n209: - Confirm the prompt still contains the static rubric and the same decision contract.\n210: - Confirm tests cover absence, malformed memory, topic filtering, cap behavior, truncation, mixed coverage, and rubric preservation.\n211: \n212: \n213: --- git provenance ---\n214: [no new commits since origin/main]\n215: \n216: --- ALLOWED SKILLS FOR ROLE MANAGER ---\n217: Available skills for this role:\n218:   - self-audit (/self-audit, audit, /inspect)\n219:   - code-review (/review, code-review, /approve)\n220:   - repo-compare (/repo-compare, /compare, /gh-compare)\n221: \n222: --- SYSTEM GENERATED & ARCHIVED ARTIFACTS ---\n223: Note: The Manager must treat the following files as system-generated or routinely archived.\n224: Deletions, moves to _archive/, or overwrites of these files are expected automated behaviors, not suspicious manual deletions:\n225: - PLAN_WP-*.md, AUDIT_WP-*.md\n226: - review_queue.md, notifications.md\n227: - archive_collaboration_artifacts.py\n228: - .session_state.json\n229: \n230: \n231: --- INSTRUCTIONS ---\n232: This review is advisory. It informs but does not replace the human operator's judgment.\n233: \n234: Judge whether the ticket OBJECTIVE and every Acceptance Criterion are actually met by the code in the diff. A criterion not addressed by the diff is NOT met, even if unrelated code looks fine.\n235: \n236: If you APPROVE, end with EXACTLY one line:\n237: DECISION: APPROVE\n238: \n239: If you request changes, you MUST emit this exact structure (headings included) before the final line:\n240: \n241: ## SUMMARY\n242: <one line: why the ticket is not approved>\n243: \n244: ## BLOCKERS\n245: - <file:line> <what is wrong> -> <what the Builder must do>\n246: \n247: ## SUGGESTIONS\n248: - <non-blocking improvement, or 'none'>\n249: \n250: DECISION: CHANGES\n251: \n252: If you cannot decide and need human intervention, end with EXACTLY:\n253: DECISION: INSPECT\n\n(End of file - total 253 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"Review code ticket WP-2026-138. Mixing None, False, and True under a falsy guard silently breaks when the method is monkeypatched or called from a legacy path. route.\"\"\"\n569: \n570:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_approve(self, tmp_path):\n571:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: APPROVE.\"\"\"\n572:         from bus.review_bridge import \"text_regex\"\n582: \n583:     def test_parse_opencode_decision_changes(self, tmp_path):\n584:         \"\"\"Test parser detects DECISION: CHANGES.\"\"\"\n585:         from bus.review_bridge import bridge._parse_opencode_decision(stdout)\n593:         assert decision == ReviewDecision.CHANGES\n594:         assert method == \"text_regex\"\n595: \n596:     def test_opencode_review_cmd_length_is_safe(self, monkeypatch, tmp_path):\n722:         \"\"\"Test the constructed command line respects the Windows CMD limit (~8k chars) by using a prompt file.\"\"\"\n723:         import subprocess\n724:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n725: \n726:         runtime_dir = tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\"\n727:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=runtime_dir)\n728:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n729: \n730:         # Create 100 dummy files to simulate a large canonical_files list\n731:         dummy_files = []\n732:         for i in range(100):\n733:             p = tmp_path / f\"dummy_file_{i}.py\"\n734:             p.write_text(\"\")\n735:             dummy_files.append(p)\n736: \n737:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_canonical_files\", lambda: dummy_files)\n738:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_active_ticket_id\", lambda: \"opencode\")\n741: \n742:         captured_cmd = []\n743: \n744:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n745:             captured_cmd.extend(cmd_args)\n746:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(args=cmd_args, returncode=0, stdout=\"DECISION: APPROVE\", stderr=\"\")\n747: \n748:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n749: \n750:         class DummySupervisor:\n751:             def transition_ticket(self, *args, **kwargs):\n752:                 pass\n753: \n754:         bridge.run_manager_review_cycle(\n755:             ticket_id=\"WP-2026-077\",\n756:             supervisor=DummySupervisor(),\n757:             timeout_seconds=5\n758:         )\n759: \n760:         import shlex\n761:         cmd_string = shlex.join(captured_cmd)\n762: \n763:         # Verify the length is well under the 6000 character safety margin\n764:         assert len(cmd_string) < 6000\n765:         # Verify that no -f flags are passed\n766:         assert \"-f\" not in captured_cmd\n767: \n768: \n769: class TestReviewPacketTransport:\n770:     \"\"\"WP-2026-126: el contexto del review va por un review packet en disco,\n771:     no por --file (flag array que consume el mensaje) ni por argv largo.\"\"\"\n772: \n773:     @staticmethod\n774:     def _bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, os_name):\n775:         from bus.review_bridge import ReviewBridge, EventBus\n776: \n777:         event_bus = EventBus(runtime_dir=tmp_path / \".agent\" / \"runtime\" / \"events\")\n778:         bridge = ReviewBridge(event_bus=event_bus, project_root=tmp_path)\n779:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_get_manager_model\", lambda: None)\n780:         monkeypatch.setattr(bridge, \"_supports_json_format\", False)\n781:         monkeypatch.setattr(\"os.name\", os_name)\n782:         return bridge\n783: \n784:     def test_writes_review_packet(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n785:         \"\"\"El contexto completo se escribe a .agent/runtime/review_packets/<ticket>_attempt-N.md.\"\"\"\n786:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n787:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n788:             subprocess,\n789:             \"run\",\n790:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n791:         )\n792: \n793:         prompt = \"CONTEXTO DE REVIEW\\n\" + (\"x\" * 8000)\n794:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n795:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n796:         )\n797: \n798:         packet = (\n799:             tmp_path\n800:             / \".agent\"\n801:             / \"runtime\"\n802:             / \"review_packets\"\n803:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n804:         )\n805:         assert packet.exists()\n806:         assert packet.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == prompt\n807: \n808:     def test_review_packet_is_separate_per_attempt(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n809:         \"\"\"Cada intento escribe su propio packet y preserva la provenance.\"\"\"\n810:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n811: \n812:         monkeypatch.setattr(\n813:             subprocess,\n814:             \"run\",\n815:             lambda c, **k: subprocess.CompletedProcess(c, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\"),\n816:         )\n817: \n818:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n819:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 1\", attempt=1, timeout_seconds=5\n820:         )\n821:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n822:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=\"PROMPT 2\", attempt=2, timeout_seconds=5\n823:         )\n824: \n825:         packet_1 = (\n826:             tmp_path\n827:             / \".agent\"\n828:             / \"runtime\"\n829:             / \"review_packets\"\n830:             / \"WP-T_attempt-1.md\"\n831:         )\n832:         packet_2 = (\n833:             tmp_path\n834:             / \".agent\"\n835:             / \"runtime\"\n836:             / \"review_packets\"\n837:             / \"WP-T_attempt-2.md\"\n838:         )\n839:         assert packet_1.exists()\n840:         assert packet_2.exists()\n841:         assert packet_1.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 1\"\n842:         assert packet_2.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\") == \"PROMPT 2\"\n843: \n844:     def test_positional_message_short_and_no_file_flag(self, tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n845:         \"\"\"El prompt posicional es corto y referencia el packet; sin --file\n846:         y sin el prompt completo en la linea de comandos.\"\"\"\n847:         bridge = self._bridge(tmp_path, monkeypatch, \"posix\")\n848: \n849:         captured = {}\n850: \n851:         def fake_run(cmd_args, **kwargs):\n852:             captured[\"cmd\"] = cmd_args\n853:             return subprocess.CompletedProcess(cmd_args, 0, \"DECISION: APPROVE\", \"\")\n854: \n855:         monkeypatch.setattr(subprocess, \"run\", fake_run)\n856: \n857:         prompt = \"PROMPT-LARGO-UNICO \" + (\"x\" * 9000)\n858:         bridge._run_opencode_review(\n859:             ticket_id=\"WP-T\", prompt=prompt, timeout_seconds=5\n860:         )\n861: \n862:         cmd = captured[\"cmd\"]\n863:         assert \"--file\" not in cmd\n864:         assert prompt not in cmd\n865:         message = cmd[-1]\n866:         assert \"WP-T\"
